listings.title: 2026 Aygaz KEY Internship Program
listings.description:
Start unlocking your potential with the Aygaz KEY Internship Program.

Aygazâ€™s energy will support your career, and your energy will inspire us.

The Aygaz KEY Internship Program welcomes you to our dynamic, and innovative environment, offering you to opportunity to explore your potential.

This opportunity is suited for students who are:

- Looking for an impactful internship experience,
- Currently in the 3rd or 4th year of undergraduate studies,
- Available to work full-time during the summer (5 days a week)

- Covered by mandatory internship insurance provided by the university,
- Curious, eager to learn and open to new ideas,
- Confident in English and digital skills.

The application deadline is April 20.

We look forward to your applications!

listings.title: AbbVie Xperience Long Term Internship Program: Allergan Aesthetics Medical Intern
listings.description:
Are you a student who thrives on challenges and never gives up?

If so, weâ€™d love to hear from you.

Join the AbbVie Allergan Aesthetics Turkey Medical Team and contribute to projects in medical digitization, global material adaptation, medical content support, and operational processes.

listings.title: Brisa - SatÄ±ÅŸ YÃ¶netici AdayÄ± ProgramÄ± - TalentScore | Youthall
listings.description:
We support womenâ€™s presence in workforce. This program is designed to aim at women who are interested in building a career in sales management.

If you are ready to take on new adventures, are open-minded and enthusiastic; if you have observer character, this program is for you!

What will your responsibilities be during this journey?

- You will be reporting to Region Sales Manager.
- You will be provided with the opportunity to assume responsibility for projects you are interested and you will receive support.
- You will be supported with well-designed training programs and mentorship.

If you have checked all the items on your journey list and are ready for the tasks that wait for you, we can drive on with courage towards the future of work with you.

What do you need before you hit the road?

- Bachelorâ€™s degree in Engineering/Social Sciences or relevant fields.
- Maximum 2 years of experience.
- Ability to establish effective communication and business partnership with dealers.
- Ability to evaluate development opportunities in the region, to closely follow the market dynamics.
- No obstacle to travel.
- Driving license.
- Excellent written and oral communication skills.

What awaits you on your Brisa Journey?

- You can continue your journey with hybrid and new working models that fit with the future of work.
- You can get new experiences by working cross-functionally.
- You can add new learnings to your life journey through training programs designed for you for your own Brisa Journey
- You can participate in many programs, contributing to your physical and mental health.
- You can take a break on your birthday.

If you have checked all the items on your journey list, you can drive on with courage to the future of work with us!

listings.title: Demand Planner
listings.description: Job Description About the Position To be the responsible for the Demand Planning/ SNOP routines and AOP budget of the commercial units in Turkey. Lead the forecasting cycle with Commercial & Marketing & Finance teams. Been a focus point for long and short term demand discussion to drive sales in the right direction. Owner of the forecast accuracy and BIAS KPIs as well an enabler for Top Line results to be materialized. Key Accountabilities Lead SNOP (forecasting) cycle with Commercial & Marketing and Finance teams. Ensure collaboration within supply chain and commercial teams in demand planning operations. Identify demand forecast risks and develop effective mitigation plans. Develop demand plan based on business trends and demand patterns. Analyze and troubleshoot demand related issues in a timely and accurate manner. Assist in developing annual budgets and cost-effectiveness initiatives. Maintain training materials and organize trainings to team members. Follow and enforce company rules and regulations. Develop routine and ad-hoc demand related reports for management. Schedule demand review meetings with management on regular basis. Owner of Forecast Accuracy and BIAS KPIs and maintain demand planning processes to improve forecast accuracy. Work with multifunctional team to plan, coordinate and execute demand planning activities. Update forecasts for new products, seasonal products, discounts, promotions. Coordinate with inventory team to monitor and maintain optimal inventory levels. Requirements Degree in Engineering or Business Management is preferable 1-3 years working experience in demand roles English level â€“ advanced SAP knowledge is a plus Ability to risk assess and make decisions Work as a Team, team routines, engage with peers' and stakeholders, absorbs feedback to improve. Inspire Excellence, use techniques, action-oriented, getting things done We Own It, focused, task orientation Communication and Interpersonal Skills Analytical and problem-solving ability. Detail-oriented Ability to lead and motivate the team Persistence and ability to influence others The ability to facilitate change What We Offer You A fast career track like only few other companies can match. Always room for new ideas; if you have an excellent idea, please let us know and we can set it in action! About Us The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. Weâ€™re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands. listings.title: Long Term Intern (Consultancy)
listings.description: Responsibilities: What Will You Do? Project Support: Support the planning and coordination of various HR-related projects to ensure high-quality delivery and timely compliance with project requirements. Data Analytics and Insights: Assist in collecting, analyzing, and generating insights from data to support / facilitate decision-making processes for our clients. Client Exposure: Observe client meetings to understand their needs and priorities, supporting the team in managing client expectations throughout the project lifecycle. Reporting and Presentation: Contribute to preparing project deliverables and presentations, supporting the team in sharing strategic recommendations and reporting results. Industry Research: Conduct research on sector trends and best practices in HR, contributing relevant insights to ongoing projects. Solution Development: Support the development of creative and effective solutions to address client challenges, contributing to their strategic goals. Qualifications: What Are We Looking For? 4th year university student or recent graduate in Engineering, Business Administration, Economics, Psychology or related fields (preferably) - Internship experience is a plus Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Excellent communication and presentation abilities Team player with a strong willingness to learn and grow in consulting Ability to think critically and strategically Detail-oriented with a focus on quality and results Curiosity about consultancy, especially in organizational design, HR systems, and performance management What We Offer We adapt conventional global management consulting experience into a new generation working experience and offer the opportunity to work with an experienced and specialized team that stands out in the industry. Availability to work full-time or minimum 3 days per week, depending on academic schedule. As a young and rapidly growing consulting company, we offer a modern consulting experience focused on innovation, empathy and productivity, where youâ€™ll be part of a forward-thinking environment, contributing to impactful projects. The opportunity to be part of a dynamic and innovative company where you can contribute while learning how consulting projects are designed and delivered. A strong learning environment where you can discover your potential and develop foundational consulting skills. Collaboration with leading companies across various industries. Flexible working options and personalized development support. About Us Consult Up, founded in 2017, provides organizational, process, and system solutions to companies across various industries. We work with leading firms in Turkey and worldwide, offering consultancy services in workforce planning, human resources systems, and performance management. Our projects focus on strategic workforce management, organizational structuring, employee experience, and talent management. We combine global best practices with technological advancements to deliver sustainable solutions that make a lasting impact. Our approach is client-centered, always involving key stakeholders and creating guides to ensure long-term usability. Join us and become part of a team that values innovation, analytics, and client-driven results. We aim to create signature projects that make a real difference for both organizations and individuals. listings.title: Global Expansion Intern
listings.description: Founded in 2012, Obilet.com is a leading travel and tourism platform offering comprehensive services, including bus, flight, and ferry ticketing, along with hotel bookings and car rentals. As the undisputed market leader in Turkey across both B2B and B2C segments, the platform supports bus carriers with its best-in-class SaaS Inventory Management System (IMS), attracts 25 million monthly visitors, and boasts 15 million app downloads. Projected to mediate over 50 million transactions in 2025, Obilet is expanding internationally to become one of the largest global players in the travel tech industry. Main responsibilities will include: Support our global expansion team Create strategies with the global expansion team Research and analyze competitors, industry trends, and markets Analyze ongoing business developments, identify relevant teams and ensure effective communication Support all operations and projects of all foreign country businesses To be successful in this role, you need to have: Strong analytical capabilities Fluency in English both written and oral Person interested in new products, tracking trends Excellent knowledge of Google Sheets & Microsoft Office Programs Very good communication and interpersonal skills AI project or experienced is nice to have What we offer: ğŸ  Hybrid Working: We work hybrid, but our offices are always open for us ğŸ‚ Birthday Leave: In the month of our birthday, we can take a day off on any day we choose ğŸ“… Flexible Working Hours: On the days we come to the office, we set our own start time with our flexible working model ğŸ‰ Happy Hours & Company Events: With our New Yearâ€™s Event, #obiletfest and Happy Hours, we not only strive for success but also have a lot of fun along the way ğŸ“š Self-Development: Your growth is our focus, with access to online learning platforms like Udemy and various training programs designed to support your professional development ğŸŒŸ Best-in-class Teammates: We are all the best in our fields and we are confident about it ğŸ¤  No Dress Code: We have no dress code. listings.title: Driver Operations Intern
listings.description: Lalamove is disrupting the logistics industry by connecting customers and drivers directly through our technology. We offer customers a lightning-fast and convenient way to book delivery and moving services whether they are at home, at work, or on the go. Currently, Lalamove is a leading global on-demand delivery platform with millions of delivery partners serving millions of orders every day. With 1600+ employees spread across SEA and LATAM, our 12-year-old company reached unicorn status in 2018, is well-funded by prominent VCs, and has kept growing at tremendous speed since. Our strength lies in our internal values: Passion in serving local communities, empowering SMEs, and our driver partners; Execution and Grit because that is how we differentiate ourselves by never giving up and striving for excellence; and Humility â€“ awareness in ourselves to learn from others and never stop improving. We are looking for a Driver Operations Intern to join our growing Marketing Team in Turkey!. The role is ideal for students who enjoy working with data, problem-solving, and supporting operational processes. Join us and be part of Lalamoveâ€™s exciting journey in Turkey! ğŸ’› What You Will Do? Assist in day-to-day driver operations and support operational efficiency Collect, analyze, and interpret operational data to identify trends and insights Collaborate with the team to optimize processes and resolve operational issues Support reporting and dashboard creation in Excel and other MS Office tools What We Seek? Third- or fourth-year undergraduate student in Business Administration, Economics, Industrial Engineering, or related fields Fluent in written and spoken English Strong analytical and problem-solving skills P roficient in MS Office applications, especially Excel Able to understand and interpret data effectively Good communication skills and ability to work in a team Curious, creative, and result-oriented with a growth mindset Highly self-motivated, flexible, and energetic To all candidates: Lalamove respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data. This Notice will inform you how we will use your personal data, explain your privacy rights, and the protection you have by the law when you apply to join us. Please take time to read and understand this Notice. listings.title: HTalent 2026 Summer Term Internship Program
listings.description:
HTalent is a short-term summer internship program which seeks high motivated university students to offer them a real time work experiences in our company. During this 20-day period , you will discover & demonstrate your competencies with the assigned project.

What kind of talents we are looking for?

-
Undergraduate degree university students from the related departments (Engineering, Business Administration, Social Sciences or related field.)

-
Have a minimum 2.5 or higher GPA

-
Have strong communication skills in English (written & spoken)

-
Self-driven, proactive, eager to learn and strong team player

-
Able to reside in Kocaeli / Ä°stanbul during the internship period.

In which locations and departments can HTalents work?

Ä°zmit Plant:
Production (Press, Body, Paint, Assembly), Production Operation (Engineering, Production Management, Part Supply management), Production Support (Maintenance, Facility), Quality (Quality Control, Advanced Quality, Quality Assurance), Information & Communication Technologies, People & Culture, Finance (Cost Analysis, Cost Accounting), Corporate Communication, General Affairs, Procurement (Part Development, Purchasing)

Ä°stanbul Office:
People & Culture, Sales, After Sales, Marketing, Finance (Business Analysis, Corporate Accounting), Information & Communication Technologies

Please note that:
Mandatory internship insurance must be covered by university.

With this program you will have the chance to:

-
Experience in a global level job environment and recognized internationally

-
Boost your creativity and innovative thinking through project presentation

-
Expand your perspective with inspiring executive talks

-
Build your professional network and take a confident step toward your future career

Internship Periods:

Izmit Plant:
13 July - 10 August / 11 August - 7 September

Istanbul Office:
15 June - 10 July / 13 July - 10 August / 11 August - 7 September

Do you desire to reach your full potential? Now it's your turn, click the apply button!

Don't forget! The last application date is 18th of April 2026.

listings.title: Experience McKinsey Summer Program
listings.description:
Curious what does McKinsey do anyway? Are you passionate about problem-solving and navigating complex situations? If yes, we invite you to join us for the Experience McKinsey Summer Program and make the first step towards entering the exciting world of consulting.

During this three-day program, you will get first-hand insights on how you can sharpen your problem-solving, presentation and communication skills, as well as stretch your imagination and come up with innovative solutions to the day-to-day challenges faced by McKinsey consultants. Additionally, you will be able to meet colleagues from our Istanbul office, hear personal stories from our consultants, and get answers to your questions.

Everyone has a unique story, we want to hear yours.

What to expect

Program

During June 24th-26th, we will give you an insiderâ€™s look into McKinsey Istanbul. You will be coached on how to best leverage your leadership and communication skills, and will work collaboratively with our consultants in small teams on a real-life business case, similar to the one you may experience during the consulting interviews.

We manufacture and market airbags, seatbelts and steering wheels. This internship offers practical, handsâ€‘on experience in process engineering activities, project work and continuous improvement within a manufacturing environment. Assist in the development and implementation of standard work, process flows and work instructions to improve efficiency and quality. Support continuous improvement projects using Lean tools, Poke Yoke, root cause analysis (8D) and dataâ€‘driven problem solving. Collaborate with crossâ€‘functional teams (production, maintenance, quality, project management) and report progress to project owners and management. listings.title: Sales Excellence Intern
listings.description: Job Description About the Position We have an excellent opportunity available for a Sales Excellence Intern to make a difference at Kraft Heinz Turkey. The Sales Excellence Intern will support the Sales Excellence team in routine reporting, data cleaning, and sales & trade marketing operations. This role aims to improve data accuracy, reporting efficiency, and operational discipline across commercial processes. The position offers hands-on exposure to sales analytics, commercial performance management, and cross-functional collaboration within a fast-paced FMCG environment. Key Responsibilities Assist in the preparation of regular sales reports (sell-in, sell-out, KPI tracking, performance dashboards). Support data consolidation from multiple sources, including distributor files and internal systems Perform data cleaning, validation, and reconciliation to ensure reporting accuracy. Maintain and update recurring Excel-based reporting templates under the guidance of the Sales Analyst. Support ad-hoc analytical requests as needed. Support tracking of trade marketing and sales operations activities (allocations, basic spend tracking, customer performance). Assist in operational coordination related to trade materials, sampling, and supply requests. Maintain basic trackers for trade marketing execution. Support trade marketing field executions in Istanbul. Assist in preparing presentation materials (charts, tables, summaries) for internal meetings. Provide general administrative and analytical support to the team when required. Qualifications 4th-year undergraduate student or recent graduate in Industrial Engineering, Business Administration,Economics or related fields. Strong Excel skills (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, basic formulas). High attention to detail and data accuracy. Comfortable working with large datasets. Strong analytical mindset with structured thinking. High sense of ownership and responsibility for assigned tasks. Good communication skills both English and Turkish and willingness to ask clarifying questions. Ability to manage deadlines, prioritize workload, and follow up on long-term initiatives What We Offer Hands-on experience in Sales Excellence and Commercial Analytics. Exposure to real-life FMCG sales processes and distributor environments. Practical understanding of reporting governance, data discipline, and performance management. Opportunity to work closely with Sales, Trade Marketing, and Finance stakeholders. About Us The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. Weâ€™re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands. No matter the brand, weâ€™re united under one vision To sustainably grow by delighting more consumers globally. Bringing this vision to life is our team of 39,000+ food lovers, creative thinkers, and high performers worldwide. Together, we help provide meals to those in need through our global partnership with Rise Against Hunger. We also stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, weâ€™re transforming the food industry with bold thinking and unprecedented results. If you share our passion â€“ and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen â€“ thereâ€™s only one thing to do join our table and letâ€™s make life delicious! Our Culture of Ownership, Meritocracy and Collaboration We're not afraid to think differently. Embrace new ideas. Dream big. We empower our people at every level â€“ from entry-level intern to senior leader â€“ to own their work. We share a responsibility to think like Owners â€“ to be mindful of the collective and sustained success of Kraft Heinz â€“ which we apply to every situation, every day. As part of Kraft Heinz, you're supported to grow and achieve. Youâ€™re expected to bring your authentic self to work every day, to lead with humility, and drive outstanding performance at every level â€“ and youâ€™ll be rewarded. Youâ€™re given opportunities to leave a mark and build a legacy. But you wonâ€™t do it alone. Youâ€™re supported by passionate teammates along the way, and our collective, collaborative spirit fuels our incredible progress. Employee Candidate Privacy Notice https //www.heinz.eu/turkey-candidate-privacy-policy We are proud to be a Great Place to Work certified company. Click to view our certificate https //www.greatplacetowork.com.tr/sertifikasyon/sertifikali-sirketler/kraft-heinz-turkiye/ Location(s) Istanbul Branch Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer â€“ Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes. Through our leadership in connected lighting and the Internet of Things, we're breaking new ground in data analytics, AI, and smart solutions for homes, offices, cities, and beyond. At Signify, you can shape tomorrow by building on our incredible 125+ year legacy while working toward even bolder sustainability goals. Weâ€™re greater together through peer learning in our globally diverse team, with different backgrounds and nationalities. Weâ€™re looking for a Lighting Design Long-Term Intern whoâ€™ll be responsible for providing lighting design outcomes and support. Youâ€™ll be part of the Signify Turkey Lighting Design Center. What youâ€™ll do Support to develop lighting concepts Prepare of lighting calculations Prepare of technical documentation Prepare visualizations and presentations of proposed lighting solutions to customers and end users What youâ€™ll need Final year students in bachelor or master programs Study preference in the area of electrical/electronics engneering, architecture, interior architecture, landscape architecture, industrial design or equivalents Fluent English and Turkish (both written and spoken) is obligatory Active Autocad software usage is obligatory Knowledge of Dialux, Dialux Evo, Relux, Photoshop, 3D studio, Revit, Lumiom will be an added value Willingness to grow in transforming lighting industry Creativity; excellent communication as well as interpersonal skills Ability to interact with others in a professional, courteous and ethical manner Flexible team player and self-motivated What we promise Weâ€™re committed to the continuous development of our employees, using our learning to shape the future of light and create a sustainable future. Join the undisputed leader in the lighting industry and be part of our diverse global team. #WeAreSignify #SignifyLife Everything weâ€™ll do for you You can grow a lasting career here. Weâ€™ll encourage you, support you, and challenge you. Weâ€™ll help you learn and progress in a way thatâ€™s right for you, with coaching and mentoring along the way. Weâ€™ll listen to you too, because we see and value every one of our 27,000+ people. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace fosters creativity, innovation, and a full spectrum of bright ideas. With a global workforce present in 70+ countries, we are dedicated to creating an inclusive environment where every voice is heard and valued, helping us all achieve more together. listings.title: Supplier Quality Manager (Site)
listings.description: Fantastic challenges. Amazing opportunities. GKN Aerospace is reimagining air travel: going further, faster and greener! Fuelled by great people whose expertise and creativity sets the standards in our industry, weâ€™re inspired by the opportunities to innovate and break boundaries. Weâ€™re proud to play a part in protecting the worldâ€™s democracies. And weâ€™re committed to putting sustainability at the centre of everything we do, opening up and protecting our planet. With over 16,000 employees across 33 manufacturing sites in 12 countries we serve over 90% of the worldâ€™s aircraft and engine manufacturers and achieved sales of Â£3.35 bn.in 2023. There are no limits to where you can take your career. Job Summary To be functionally responsible for all Supplier quality activities, within the Site Procurement organisation. This role requires strong people leadership and resource management skills to deliver against performance objectives focused on continuous improvement, people development and supplier quality performance. Job Responsibilities All Quality Assurance activities within the Procurement Business Assures that the Quality system is adequate and is being applied and adhered to within the business, through the provision of independently gathered evidence by the business. Assures that the certification processes within the business is adequately defined, accurate, credible, robust and consistently applied. Driving Supplier Quality Improvement Plans within the Function to achieve Global Procurement and department targets (i.e. escapes reduction plans and concession (tags) reduction plans) Contact point for partners / internal customer representatives, facilitating / managing customer inspection. Ensuring that competent Manufacturing Engineers accurately convert the customer requirements in to clear supplier instructions. Accuracy of the Quality data set. Acting as a focal point for Quality issues relating to the supply of product. Driving supplier process improvements Developing and deploying performance and process metrics that are consistent across the Global Procurement organisation. Ensure the Health and Safety policies and procedure are adhered to by the Procurement Quality team; Accountable for the management of the people within the Procurement Quality team ensuring that PDP, development plans and succession planning is completed; Ensuring that the people within the Procurement Quality team have the adequate skills to carry out their roles, where skill gaps are identified, putting training and development plans in place to address them. Ensuring adherence to all applicable regulatory, legal, and quality requirements. Job Qualifications Weâ€™ll offer you fantastic challenges and amazing opportunities. This is your chance to be part of an organisation that has proven itself to be at the cutting edge of our industry; and is committed to pushing the boundaries even further. And with some of the best training on offer in the industry, who knows how far you can go? A Great Place to work needs a Great Way of Working Everyone is welcome to apply to GKN. We believe that we can only achieve our ambitions through a coming together of diverse minds who enjoy collaborating in an inspirational environment. Through our commitment to diversity, inclusion and belonging and by living our five powerful principles weâ€™ve created a culture where everyone feels welcome to contribute. Itâ€™s a culture that won us â€˜The Best Workplace Culture Awardâ€™. By embracing and celebrating what makes us unique we encourage everyone to bring their full self to work. Weâ€™re also committed to providing an accessible recruitment process, so if you require reasonable adjustments at any stage during our recruitment process please get in touch and let us know. We are the place where human dreams, plus human endeavour, shape the future of aerospace innovation and technology. Show more Show less

listings.title: Contractor Quality Lead
listings.description: Role Description: The Global External Quality Operations Mission is â€œto be a proactive partner, driving a science and risk-based compliant quality culture that is flexible, innovative, and customer oriented, maximizing the contribution of Quality Operations to Our Path Forward initiatives.â€ External Supply Operations Quality (ESOQ) is committed to supporting Pfizer Global Supply (PGS) as an innovative and competitive advantage for Pfizer by assuring the quality of products manufactured and packaged by other companies (Contractors) for Pfizer. ESOQ is responsible for the Quality & Regulatory management of approximately 300 contract manufacturers, packagers, and supply partners globally. As a Senior Manager, the advanced and comprehensive knowledge of the principles and concepts of GMP and of other disciplines will enable the function to lead complex projects across the division and support /back up the ESOQ Quality Operations Team Lead as needed. Some regional specific roles may have region specific responsibilities. Accountabilities Actively contributes as part of the ESOQ Tier 2 structure to ensure that Pfizer network of External suppliers / CMOâ€™s / partners are supported to deliver high quality products, on time, and in compliance with all established standards and agency guidelines/ applicable GMP regulations. Responsibilities: Manage all aspects related to product quality or compliance for a complex portfolio of external contract manufacturers and suppliers that supply Product(s) globally Assess the quality of external supplierâ€™s products, processes and related documents while ensuring the product specifications are met and quality systems are maintained. Support internal and external partners with auditing activities related to ESOQ activities, or activities at a portfolio of contractors - (RQA audits or regulatory inspections) as applicable Support internal and external partners with Compliance Assessments to ensure current GMP/GDP adherence Manage Risk at Vendors implementing Quality Improvement Plans and support Risk assessments, QRTs and QANs as necessary Support onboarding activities of new CMOâ€™s or product launches within the portfolio as applicable. Support offboarding activities for exiting CMOâ€™s/products/SKUs within the portfolio as applicable Make product Quality decisions leveraging advanced knowledge of Quality/Manufacturing principles, prior work experience and concepts in other technical areas while ensuring compliance to global regulatory and Pfizer requirements. Ability to lead and influence quality decision making across all levels of the organization and with our external partners. Provide Quality Leadership within the cross functional virtual site operating teams (VSOT). Partner with colleagues to develop and negotiate quality agreements. Review and approve Annual Product Review and Product Quality Review (APR/PQR) reports and relevant product stability reports. Perform batch disposition endorsement. Identify, develop and implement continuous improvement initiatives related to ESOQ processes to ensure compliance with the latest internal and external standards. Ensure the adequate tracking and documentation of all required quality related actions in the relevant system(s) and that all required escalation processes are followed. Lead and support complex investigations, market complaints, risk assessments and others in support of products within your portfolio. Raise NTMs and connect with SMEs where needed. Provide Performance Metrics for key performance indicators. Work independently and operate with minimal supervision, interact with high levels of management both at contract manufacturers and within Pfizer. Build, maintain and develop relationships with key stakeholders, both internal and external to Pfizer. Support the development, implementation and continuous improvement of innovative Quality Systems. Be a resource for manager level colleagues on the team and peers in External Supply Quality and be able to provide directions to peers including risk-based decision making and innovative solutions to ambiguous problems. Develop ideas and lead complex crossfunctional project teams including internal business and manufacturing organizations (e.g. External Supply, Regulatory, Technical Support, Quality, PGS sites and R&D), anticipate potential challenges the team may encounter, and be responsible for the teamâ€™s deliverables and milestones. Show interest in Innovation and innovative approaches. Support ESOQ Leadership In Other Tasks As Required. Support and act as back up of the team Site Quality Operations Lead as required. If applicable: Managing all aspects related to product quality or compliance for a portfolio of external contract manufacturers (CMO), contract testing organizations (CTO) and suppliers that supply materials/product(s) to Pfizer according to established procedures. Creation of Process Orders (POs) and support to define provisions within the SOW/MSA for QC testing execution at the CTO. Ensure stability studies at CMO/CTO sites are conducted according to approved protocols and ICH guidance. Qualifications: Minimally Bachelor of Science Degree, Pharmacy, Engineering, or related technical discipline Minimally 10 yearsâ€™ experience in Pharmaceutical or Medical Device Quality Assurance roles with direct responsibility for product quality decisions and /or 15 yearsâ€™ experience in the pharmaceutical or medical device industry. Experience with external suppliers/contractors. Advanced computer skills for MSOffice and enterprise systems such SAP, QTS, Vault, Documentum platforms, Minitab Proficient in English and technical writing Strong verbal, written communication, and presentation skills Demonstrated personal leadership to lead virtual teams and cross functional projects/initiatives Is diplomatic, clear & succinct in communication with internal and external stakeholders Demonstrated experience managing complex quality and compliance activities Demonstrated ability to resolve conflicts Demonstrated managerial/organizational skills Self-motivator & proven ability to lead independently Strong organizing and planning skills and a high sense of urgency Demonstrated knowledge of US, European and global cGMPs, compliance issues, inspectional trends, industry quality assurance practices Proven leadership/facilitation skills and being able to involve several levels of an organization to successfully meet the objectives Work Location Assignment: Hybrid GJL: J090 Worker Type: Regular Pfizer is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable equal employment opportunity legislation in each jurisdiction in which it operates. Quality Assurance and Control Show more Show less

Support internal and external partners with auditing activities related to ESOQ activities, or activities at a portfolio of contractors - (RQA audits or regulatory inspections) as applicable Support internal and external partners with Compliance Assessments to ensure current GMP/GDP adherence Manage Risk at Vendors implementing Quality Improvement Plans and support Risk assessments, QRTs and QANs as necessary Support onboarding activities of new CMOâ€™s or product launches within the portfolio as applicable. Support offboarding activities for exiting CMOâ€™s/products/SKUs within the portfolio as applicable Make product Quality decisions leveraging advanced knowledge of Quality/Manufacturing principles, prior work experience and concepts in other technical areas while ensuring compliance to global regulatory and Pfizer requirements. Ability to lead and influence quality decision making across all levels of the organization and with our external partners. Provide Quality Leadership within the cross functional virtual site operating teams (VSOT). Partner with colleagues to develop and negotiate quality agreements. Review and approve Annual Product Review and Product Quality Review (APR/PQR) reports and relevant product stability reports. Perform batch disposition endorsement. Identify, develop and implement continuous improvement initiatives related to ESOQ processes to ensure compliance with the latest internal and external standards. Ensure the adequate tracking and documentation of all required quality related actions in the relevant system(s) and that all required escalation processes are followed. Lead and support complex investigations, market complaints, risk assessments and others in support of products within your portfolio. Raise NTMs and connect with SMEs where needed. Provide Performance Metrics for key performance indicators. Work independently and operate with minimal supervision, interact with high levels of management both at contract manufacturers and within Pfizer. Build, maintain and develop relationships with key stakeholders, both internal and external to Pfizer. Support the development, implementation and continuous improvement of innovative Quality Systems. Be a resource for manager level colleagues on the team and peers in External Supply Quality and be able to provide directions to peers including risk-based decision making and innovative solutions to ambiguous problems. Develop ideas and lead complex crossfunctional project teams including internal business and manufacturing organizations (e.g. External Supply, Regulatory, Technical Support, Quality, PGS sites and R&D), anticipate potential challenges the team may encounter, and be responsible for the teamâ€™s deliverables and milestones. Show interest in Innovation and innovative approaches. Support ESOQ Leadership In Other Tasks As Required. Support and act as back up of the team Site Quality Operations Lead as required. If applicable: Managing all aspects related to product quality or compliance for a portfolio of external contract manufacturers (CMO), contract testing organizations (CTO) and suppliers that supply materials/product(s) to Pfizer according to established procedures. Creation of Process Orders (POs) and support to define provisions within the SOW/MSA for QC testing execution at the CTO. Ensure stability studies at CMO/CTO sites are conducted according to approved protocols and ICH guidance. Qualifications: Minimally Bachelor of Science Degree, Pharmacy, Engineering, or related technical discipline Minimally 10 yearsâ€™ experience in Pharmaceutical or Medical Device Quality Assurance roles with direct responsibility for product quality decisions and /or 15 yearsâ€™ experience in the pharmaceutical or medical device industry. Experience with external suppliers/contractors. Advanced computer skills for MSOffice and enterprise systems such SAP, QTS, Vault, Documentum platforms, Minitab Proficient in English and technical writing Strong verbal, written communication, and presentation skills Demonstrated personal leadership to lead virtual teams and cross functional projects/initiatives Is diplomatic, clear & succinct in communication with internal and external stakeholders Demonstrated experience managing complex quality and compliance activities Demonstrated ability to resolve conflicts Demonstrated managerial/organizational skills Self-motivator & proven ability to lead independently Strong organizing and planning skills and a high sense of urgency Demonstrated knowledge of US, European and global cGMPs, compliance issues, inspectional trends, industry quality assurance practices Proven leadership/facilitation skills and being able to involve several levels of an organization to successfully meet the objectives Work Location Assignment: Hybrid GJL: J090 Worker Type: Regular Pfizer is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable equal employment opportunity legislation in each jurisdiction in which it operates. listings.title: Human Resources Intern
listings.description: Are you eager to build a career in Human Resources and ready to apply your theoretical knowledge in a real business environment? Join our HR team! The CMA CGM Group , a global leader in shipping and logistics, serves more than 420 ports around the world on five continents. Present in 160 countries through its network of more than 400 offices and 750 warehouses, the Group employs more than 155,000 people worldwide, including 4,000 in Marseilles where its head office is located. With its subsidiary CEVA Logistics, a world leader in logistics, and its air freight division CMA CGM AIR CARGO, the CMA CGM Group is continually innovating to offer its customers a complete and increasingly efficient range of new shipping, land, air, and logistics solutions. As a result of expanding our organization, we are looking for a Human Resources Intern to join our fast-growing and challenging organization. Key Responsibilities: Support recruitment and onboarding processes, Assist with CV screening and interview scheduling, Take part in ongoing HR projects, Support internal communication and documentation tasks within the department, Assist with departmental purchases and invoice processing. What Weâ€™re Looking For: 3rd or 4th-year university students from relevant departments (Psychology, Business Administration, HR Management, etc.), Good command of English, both verbal and written, Available to work full-time in the summer period at the office in Istanbul, Strong communication skills, Problem-solving and adaptability in a dynamic work environment, Eager to learn. What We Offer: Real hands-on experience in a corporate environment A dynamic and supportive team Breakfast: every Monday morning Transportation (Shuttle) Meal allowance Happy hours Valuable learning and networking opportunities for your future career During the recruitment process, the personal data you provide in your CV is processed for purposes including contacting you, implementing human resources policies and procedures, managing candidate selection and placement processes, conducting reference checks, and ensuring security. For more detailed information about how your data is processed, please refer to our Employee Candidate Privacy Notice. We have zero tolerance for inequality and are deeply committed to fostering Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DE&I) at every level of our organization. As HR and business partners, we actively drive and support initiatives that build a more inclusive and equitable workplace for all. Come along on CMA CGM's adventure! Show more Show less

listings.title: Intern
listings.description: Requisition ID: 36595 Office Country: TÃ¼rkiye Office City: Istanbul Division: Banking Countries of Operations Contract Type: Intern Contract Length: 06 months Posting End Date: 16/04/2026 Purpose of Job The Intern will support the COP31 coordination team in the preparation and delivery of activities related to the COP31 event to be held in Antalya, TÃ¼rkiye, in November 2026. The intern will contribute to a range of logistical, operational, and technical tasks required before and during the event, helping ensure the smooth functioning of on-the-ground activities and coordination among stakeholders. The Intern will assist in organising event logistics, coordinating with internal teams and external partners, and providing day-to-day operational support during the preparation phase as well as throughout the conference period. While supporting multiple workstreams across the teamâ€™s responsibilities, the intern will also contribute to background research, information management, and technical assistance related to event delivery. The Local Administration Team is responsible for providing operational, logistical, and administrative support for the organisation and delivery of the COP31 conference to be held in Antalya, TÃ¼rkiye, in November 2026. The team plays a central role in ensuring that the day-to-day administrative functions and on-the-ground coordination required for a large-scale international conference are carried out efficiently and in close collaboration with relevant national authorities, international partners, and conference stakeholders. Under the leadership of the Principal Administration Manager, the team manages a broad range of operational activities that support the planning, preparation, and implementation of COP31. These include coordinating local logistics, managing administrative processes, supporting procurement and vendor coordination, and facilitating communication and coordination between the conference secretariat, government counterparts, service providers, and other participating organisations. The team is also responsible for overseeing routine administrative operations related to the conference preparation and delivery, including documentation management, scheduling and coordination of meetings, logistical arrangements for delegations and events, and providing practical support during the conference period to ensure smooth operations. Working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment, the Local Administration Team supports both planned activities and ad hoc operational needs that arise in the lead-up to and during the conference. The intern will work under the supervision of the Principal Administration Manager and support the team across a variety of daily and ad hoc tasks, contributing to the effective coordination and delivery of COP31-related administrative and logistical activities. Accountabilities & Responsibilities Provide comprehensive administrative and operational support to the team on the ground and to relevant HQ functions to contribute to the smooth planning and delivery of the COP31 event in Antalya, TÃ¼rkiye. Work closely with both internal colleagues and external stakeholders, including service providers, partner institutions, and conference counterparts, to support coordination and information flow. Assist the team with logistical coordination and day-to-day administrative tasks related to COP31 preparation and implementation, including documentation, scheduling, and meeting coordination. Provide occasional administrative and coordination support to other related project activities and workstreams as required. Support the team with ad hoc tasks and special assignments that arise in the context of conference planning and operations. Assist in monitoring relevant developments, operational updates, and information related to COP31 planning and implementation. Attend internal and external meetings or events when appropriate and provide brief summaries or notes to support team coordination. Contribute to effective communication and coordination across teams by helping track actions, maintain documentation, and support information management processes. Knowledge, Skills, Experience & Qualifications Fluency in English; Excellent writing skills in English and an eye for quality and detail Communication skills â€“ Ability to communicate appropriate, concise and accurate information in verbal and written formats, appropriate to the audience at the time Strong research skills â€“ Ability to research and distill information with speed and efficiency Computer literacy, conversant with Microsoft Office and spread sheet packages. Ability to work independently while also knowing when to seek advice; Ability to operate in a multi-cultural environment with changing priorities. Strong Team player University student or graduates within the last 3 years are suitable. What is it like to work at the EBRD? / About EBRD Our agile and innovative approach is what makes life at the EBRD a unique experience! You will be part of a pioneering and diverse international organisation, and use your talents to make a real difference to people's lives and help shape the future of the regions we invest in. At EBRD, our Values â€“ Inclusiveness, Innovation, Trust, and Responsibility â€“ are at the heart of how we work. We bring these to life through our Workplace Behaviours: listening well and speaking up, collaborating smartly, acting decisively with full commitment, and simplifying to amplify our impact. These principles shape our culture and define our success. We seek individuals who not only share these values but are also committed to embedding them in their daily work, fostering a positive and high-performing environment. The EBRD Environment Provides You With Varied, stimulating and engaging work that gives you an opportunity to interact with a wide range of experts in the financial, political, public and private sectors across the regions we invest in. A working culture that embraces inclusion and celebrates diversity. Our workforce reflects a broad range of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences, bringing fresh ideas, energy, and innovation and enhancing our ability to serve our clients, shareholders, and counterparties effectively. A hybrid workplace that offers flexibility to teams and individuals; that is based on trust, flexibility and connectedness. An environment that places sustainability, equality and digital transformation at the heart of what we do. A workplace that prioritises employee wellbeing and provides a comprehensive suite of competitive benefits. Diversity is one of the Bankâ€™s core values which are at the heart of everything it does. As such, the EBRD seeks to ensure that everyone is treated with respect and given equal opportunities and works in an inclusive environment. The EBRD encourages all qualified candidates who are nationals of the EBRD member countries to apply regardless of their racial, ethnic, religious and cultural background, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, socio-economic background or disability. Please note, that due to the high volume of applications received, we regret to inform you that we are unable to provide detailed feedback to candidates who have not been shortlisted (for further consideration). Show more Show less

listings.title: Customer Service & Logistics Trainee
listings.description: Site Name: Istanbul River Plaza Posted Date: Mar 27 2026 We manufacture and supply reliable, high-quality medicines and vaccines to meet patientsâ€™ needs and drive our performance.Our network of 37 medicines and vaccines manufacturing sites delivered 1.7 billion packs of medicines and 409 million vaccine doses in 2024 to help make a positive impact on the health of millions of people. Our supply chain is not just core to our operations; itâ€™s vital to bringing our innovations to patients as quickly, efficiently and effectively as possible. Technology is transforming how we manufacture medicines and vaccines, enabling us to increase the speed, quality and scale of product supply.We need the very best minds and capability to help us on our journey to make more complex products, harnessing the power of smart manufacturing technologies including robotics, digital solutions and artificial intelligence to deliver for patients. Customer Service and Logistics Trainee You will join our TÃ¼rkiye team as a Customer Service & Logistics Trainee. You will support customer service and supply chain activities, learn operational processes, and work closely with colleagues across functions. We value curiosity, clear communication and a growth mindset. This role offers hands-on learning, coaching, and clear milestones to develop a long-term career while helping GSK unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. Responsibilities: This role will provide YOU the opportunity to lead key activities to progress YOUR career. These responsibilities include some of the following:- Support daily operational requirements. Assist with coordination of inbound and outbound logistics to meet timelines and quality standards. Help maintain accurate inventory and shipment records in our systems. Prepare simple reports and presentations for internal stakeholders. Work with cross-functional teams to resolve routine supply issues and improve processes. Participate in training and development activities, applying feedback to improve performance. Why You? This role is based in TÃ¼rkiye and is an on-site position. You will join a supportive team that values inclusion, practical learning and clear development paths. You will receive regular coaching and have opportunities to move into more senior roles as you grow. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds. If you need adjustments during recruitment, please contact our Recruitment Team. Basic Qualification We are seeking professionals with the following required skills and qualifications to help us achieve our goals: University students of 3rd or 4th grade, in business, supply chain, logistics, engineering, life sciences or a related field. Proficient Turkish and professional working English. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Good communication skills and a collaborative mindset. Basic computer skills, including Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint). Legal right to work in TÃ¼rkiye. Preferred Qualification If you have the following characteristics, it would be a plus: Internship or practical experience in customer service, logistics, warehouse or supply chain roles. Familiarity with SAP or other ERP systems. Basic knowledge of good manufacturing and storage practices. Experience supporting cross-functional teams or project work.- Training on manual handling or forklift operation. Interest in building a career in pharmaceutical, healthcare or related sectors. How to apply If you are ready to learn, grow and make a meaningful contribution, we want to hear from you. Please submit your CV and a short cover letter explaining why you are interested in this trainee role in TÃ¼rkiye. We look forward to learning about you and the potential you bring. Why GSK? You will join a team focused on patient outcomes and scientific excellence. You will have development opportunities and access to global expertise. You will work in a supportive environment that values inclusion, respect and open feedback. If you are ready to use your clinical knowledge and relationship skills to make a difference, please apply today. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds. What we offer You will join an organisation that aims to make a real difference to patients. We invest in learning and development to help you grow. You will work with people who value inclusion and diverse perspectives. If you want to build a meaningful career in oncology and help shape better outcomes for patients, we encourage you to apply. GSK is an Equal Opportunity Employer. This ensures that all qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), parental status, national origin, age, disability, genetic information (including family medical history), military service or any basis prohibited under federal, state or local law. Why GSK? Uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. We aim to positively impact the health of 2.5 billion people by the end of the decade, as a successful, growing company where people can thrive. We get ahead of disease by preventing and treating it with innovation in specialty medicines and vaccines. We focus on four therapeutic areas: respiratory, immunology and inflammation; oncology; HIV; and infectious diseases â€“ to impact health at scale. People and patients around the world count on the medicines and vaccines we make, so weâ€™re committed to creating an environment where our people can thrive and focus on what matters most. Our culture of being ambitious for patients, accountable for impact and doing the right thing is the foundation for how, together, we deliver for patients, shareholders and our people. As an Inclusive employer, we encourage you to reach out if you need any adjustments during the recruitment process. Please contact our Recruitment Team at tr.recruitment-adjustments@gsk.com to discuss your needs. Please note should your enquiry not relate to adjustments; we will not be able to support you through these channels GSK is an Equal Opportunity Employer. This ensures that all qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), parental status, national origin, age, disability, genetic information (including family medical history), military service or any basis prohibited under federal, state or local law. Important notice to Employment businesses/ Agencies GSK does not accept referrals from employment businesses and/or employment agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site. All employment businesses/agencies are required to contact GSK's commercial and general procurement/human resources department to obtain prior written authorization before referring any candidates to GSK. The obtaining of prior written authorization is a condition precedent to any agreement (verbal or written) between the employment business/ agency and GSK. In the absence of such written authorization being obtained any actions undertaken by the employment business/agency shall be deemed to have been performed without the consent or contractual agreement of GSK. GSK shall therefore not be liable for any fees arising from such actions or any fees arising from any referrals by employment businesses/agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site. Please note that if you are a US Licensed Healthcare Professional or Healthcare Professional as defined by the laws of the state issuing your license, GSK may be required to capture and report expenses GSK incurs, on your behalf, in the event you are afforded an interview for employment. This capture of applicable transfers of value is necessary to ensure GSKâ€™s compliance to all federal and state US Transparency requirements. For more information, please visit the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) website at https://openpaymentsdata.cms.gov/ Show more Show less

listings.title: Supply Chain Process & Control Specialist
listings.description: Supply Chain Process & Control Specialist Our partner in the facilities services sector is seeking a Supply Chain Process & Control Specialist to streamline operations and build a robust internal control framework. With 5-8 years of experience, you will be the architect of standardized processes across the group. Key Responsibilities SAP Optimization: Simplify and re-configure SAP MM processes to ensure maximum efficiency. Internal Controls: Establish critical control points, including approval workflows, Purchase Order (SAS) requirements, and Goods Receipt (GR) compliance. Systems Integration: Coordinate with the IT department to lead system enhancements and automation projects. Standardization: Initiate and manage projects aimed at creating a unified supply chain structure across all group entities. What We Are Looking For Experience: 5-8 years of experience in Supply Chain Excellence, Internal Audit, or Process Design. Technical Expertise: Proficiency in SAP MM is mandatory . Process Design: Proven track record in designing and improving operational workflows. Attention to Detail: A disciplined approach with a strong "internal audit" mindset. Project Management: Ability to coordinate between business units and especially IT teams. Show more Show less

listings.title: Supply Chain Specialist
listings.description: Req ID: 516675 At Alstom, we understand transport networks and what moves people. From high-speed trains, metros, monorails, and trams, to turnkey systems, services, infrastructure, signalling and digital mobility, we offer our diverse customers the broadest portfolio in the industry. Every day, more than 80 000 colleagues lead the way to greener and smarter mobility worldwide, connecting cities as we reduce carbon and replace cars. Could you be the full-time Supply Chain Specialist in Istanbul weâ€™re looking for? Your future role Take on a new challenge and apply your logistics and supply chain expertise in a new cutting-edge field. Youâ€™ll work alongside collaborative and forward-thinking teammates. Youâ€™ll play a key role in improving end-to-end logistics flows, ensuring alignment with Quality, Cost, Delivery, Environment, and Health & Safety (QCDE) targets. Day-to-day, youâ€™ll work closely with teams across the business (Material Planning, Project Supply Chain, Warehouse, Procurement, and Manufacturing), optimize processes in our ERP system, and much more. Youâ€™ll specifically take care of supporting material planning and logistics activities, but also contribute to supply chain KPIs and drive value-added improvements across the value chain. Weâ€™ll look to you for: Supporting material planning activities, including delivery follow-up of purchase orders Arranging transport and customs operations for logistics activities Contributing to key supply chain KPIs, such as on-time delivery (OTD) and inventory monitoring Collaborating with central and regional supply chain teams to identify and implement value-added improvements Designing internal logistics processes and ensuring ERP parameters are accurate Aligning physical and system flows to improve stock accuracy Participating in procurement gate reviews and negotiating project supply chain specifications Reducing logistics costs and optimizing customs and transport operations All About You We value passion and attitude over experience. Thatâ€™s why we donâ€™t expect you to have every single skill. Instead, weâ€™ve listed some that we think will help you succeed and grow in this role: Degree in International Trade, Marketing & Logistics, Business Administration, or equivalent Experience in a supply chain environment Knowledge of ERP systems, particularly SAP (Warehouse Management and Inventory Management modules) Advanced level of English (written and spoken) Good communication skills and a team-oriented mindset Strategic and methodical vision Problem-solving skills An agile, inclusive, and responsible attitude Things youâ€™ll enjoy Join us on a life-long transformative journey â€“ the rail industry is here to stay, so you can grow and develop new skills and experiences throughout your career. Youâ€™ll also: Enjoy stability, challenges, and a long-term career free from monotonous daily routines Work with new security standards for rail signalling Collaborate with transverse teams and supportive colleagues Contribute to innovative projects Utilize our dynamic and inclusive working environment Steer your career in whatever direction you choose across functions and countries Benefit from our investment in your development, through award-winning learning Progress towards roles such as Supply Chain Manager or other leadership opportunities Benefit from a fair and dynamic reward package that recognizes your performance and potential, plus comprehensive and competitive social coverage (life, medical) You donâ€™t need to be a train enthusiast to thrive with us. We guarantee that when you step onto one of our trains with your friends or family, youâ€™ll be proud. If youâ€™re up for the challenge, weâ€™d love to hear from you! Important to note As a global business, weâ€™re an equal-opportunity employer that celebrates diversity across the 63 countries we operate in. Weâ€™re committed to creating an inclusive workplace for everyone. Job Type: Experienced Show more Show less

listings.title: Project Engineer
listings.description: We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Project Engineer to manage end-to-end project execution, including planning, analysis, budgeting, procurement, and stakeholder coordination. The role requires ownership of project timelines, cost control, and supplier interactions to ensure successful and efficient delivery. Key Responsibilities Â· Develop, maintain, and optimize detailed project schedules, including milestones, dependencies, and critical paths Â· Perform project planning, resource allocation, and capacity management Â· Conduct technical, operational, and feasibility analyses Â· Prepare project budgets, cost breakdown structures, and financial forecasts Â· Monitor and control project costs, ensuring alignment with approved budgets Â· Manage procurement processes, including supplier sourcing, evaluation, and qualification Â· Lead supplier negotiations (pricing, lead times, technical compliance, and contractual terms) Â· Execute purchasing activities such as RFQs, bid analysis, purchase orders, and contract management Â· Track project progress and ensure adherence to scope, timeline, and cost constraints Â· Identify risks, develop mitigation plans, and proactively resolve issues Â· Coordinate cross-functional teams (engineering, operations, finance, logistics) Â· Prepare regular reports, dashboards, and presentations for stakeholders and senior management Â· Ensure compliance with internal standards, quality requirements, and relevant regulations Minimum Qualifications â€¢ Bachelorâ€™s degree in Engineering (Industrial, Mechanical, Electrical, or related field) â€¢ 2+ years of experience in project engineering, project management, or similar roles â€¢ Strong understanding of project planning and scheduling methodologies â€¢ Experience with procurement, supplier management, and purchasing processes â€¢ Solid knowledge of budgeting, cost control, and financial analysis â€¢ Proficiency in MS Office tools (especially Excel and MSProject) â€¢ Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills â€¢ Effective communication and negotiation capabilities â€¢ Ability to manage multiple priorities in a deadline-driven environment Preferred Qualifications â€¢ Previous experience as a Project Engineer in defense projects, with hands-on involvement in both software and hardware domains, is a huge plus â€¢ PMP or equivalent project management certification â€¢ Experience with ERP systems â€¢ Familiarity with contract management and commercial terms â€¢ Experience in supplier negotiations and strategic sourcing â€¢ Knowledge of risk management frameworks and quality assurance processes â€¢ Exposure to multidisciplinary or international projects Benefits: Â· Competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package, including health insurance and fuel support. Â· Opportunities for continuous professional growth and skill development. Â· Engage in a collaborative and innovative work environment. Â· Enjoy a positive and enriching work environment, situated in a desirable location. Show more Show less

listings.title: Process Engineer - Utilities & Tank Farm (GME) - Relocate to Saudi Arabia Permanent Expat Family Relocation Package
listings.description: Please note that this role is based in Saudi Arabia on a permanent, residential basis. Job Req ID: 17355 Description Aramco energizes the world economy. Aramco occupies a special position in the global energy industry. We are one of the worldâ€™s largest producers of hydrocarbon energy and chemicals, with among the lowest Upstream carbon intensities of any major producer. With our significant investment in technology and infrastructure, we strive to maximize the value of the energy we produce for the world along with a commitment to enhance Aramcoâ€™s value to society. Headquartered in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and with offices around the world, we combine market discipline with a generationsâ€™ spanning view of the future, born of our nine decades experience as responsible stewards of the Kingdomâ€™s vast hydrocarbon resources. This responsibility has driven us to deliver significant societal and economic benefits to not just the Kingdom, but also to a vast number of communities, economies, and countries that rely on the vital and reliable energy that we supply. We are one of the most profitable companies in the world, as well as amongst the top five global companies by market capitalization. Overview We are seeking a Process Engineer with extensive hands-on expertise in Utilities processes to join our Operation Engineering Solutions Department (OESD) under Global Manufacturing (GM) Excellence Organization. OESD's technical team is responsible for providing high-quality, multi-disciplinary support to GM operating facilities (Refineries, NGLF, and petrochemical plants) within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and globally. Your primary is to provide in-depth process engineering and operations expertise to monitor, optimize, and drive performance improvement programs for Utilities, including both remote and on-site technical advisory support and benchmarking of utilities. Key Responsibilities As successful candidate you will be required to perform the following: Conduct regular performance reviews of existing Utilities through key performance indicators (KPIs) representing optimum operability, profitability, reliability, and process safety. Develop and present quarterly benchmarking reports with actionable improvement recommendations. Provide hands-on support in implementing performance improvement initiatives, demonstrating measurable results in closing identified gaps from quarterly benchmarking reports. Lead benchmarking and best practices exchange workshops among Global Manufacturing facilities (wholly-owned and affiliates) and facilitate knowledge sharing and continuous improvement. Deliver on-demand remote and field technical advisory support, including process design, troubleshooting, start-up/shutdown activities, and capacity test runs. Provide detailed service reports for each support activity, highlighting facility needs, actions taken, results, and lessons learned. Collaborate with Global Manufacturing facilities engineers to identify and implement cost optimization or process improvement initiatives, focusing on "low-hanging fruits" that require minimal capital investment, through operational adjustment or design modification. Conduct internal assessments on Utilities using Saudi Aramco engineering standard references and highlight any deviations or better standards applied by affiliates for endorsement to facility management or Saudi Aramco Central Engineering. Act as the overall coordinator for cascading and driving Global Manufacturing and Corporate programs for Utilities in operating facilities, enabling their execution and effective monitoring. Participate in Business Plan development, coordinate with stakeholders for capital investments on assigned Utilities and support techno-economic evaluations. Evaluate and recommend new Utilities technologies, coordinate with Central Engineering to facilitate field deployment. Minimum Requirements You will hold a Bachelor's degree in Chemical Engineering or related specialization. An advanced degree is preferred. You will have a minimum of 15 years of total Utilities unit experience (process engineering and operations) with at least 10 years of hands-on experience in handling refineryâ€™s utilities. You will have experience in water desalination and boilers for steam generation You will be able to optimize various utility systems, with specific examples of performance improvements achieved. You will have in-depth knowledge of major equipment suppliers and package units for Utilities. You will have a proven track record of conducting technical troubleshooting, start-up, shutdown, and capacity test runs for utility systems. You will have strong proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools specific to Utilities performance metrics. You will also have strong management and coordination skills, with experience in stakeholder management. Working environment Our high-performing employees are drawn by the challenging and rewarding professional, technical and industrial opportunities we offer, and are remunerated accordingly. At Aramco, our people work on truly world-scale projects, supported by investment in capital and technology that is second to none. And because, as a global energy company, we are faced with addressing some of the worldâ€™s biggest technical, logistical and environmental challenges, we invest heavily in talent development. We have a proud history of educating and training our workforce over many decades. Employees at all levels are encouraged to improve their sector-specific knowledge and competencies through our workforce development programs â€“ one of the largest in the world. Show more Show less

listings.title: Solution Center Agent (Part
listings.description: Toptalent.co | Solution Center Agent (Part-Time) - Volt Lines Ä°ÅŸ Ä°lanlarÄ± Yetenek ProgramlarÄ± Etkinlikler Sertifikalar Testler Awards Ä°ÅŸveren Ä°ÅŸveren GiriÅŸi Ãœcretsiz Ä°ÅŸ Ä°lanÄ± Ver Ä°ÅŸe AlÄ±m Ã‡Ã¶zÃ¼mleri DeÄŸerlendirme ve Ä°ÅŸe AlÄ±m Testleri Ä°K Platformu Bize UlaÅŸÄ±n | Ãœcretsiz Ãœye Ol Ãœye GiriÅŸi Ãœye Ol Ãœye GiriÅŸi Toptalent.co Ä°ÅŸ Ä°lanlarÄ± Solution Center Agent (Part-Time) Ãœcretsiz Ä°ÅŸ Ä°lanÄ± Ver Solution Center Agent (Part-Time) Son 12 GÃ¼n Volt Lines Son 12 GÃ¼n Åimdi BaÅŸvur Departman â€¢ DiÄŸer Lokasyon Ä°stanbul Avrupa Kimler BaÅŸvurabilir? Åimdi BaÅŸvur Join us and be a part of shaping the future of urban mobility! Some complain about traffic but some are actually doing something about it. Join us on our mission to reduce traffic in Istanbul by increasing the accessibility of sustainable shared mobility. Who are we? Volt Lines is the only technology-enabled player in the Turkish market competing against legacy incumbents and it reduces emissions and traffic by turning premium 16-seater buses into a viable alternative to single occupancy vehicles. What we do? In Istanbul, over 50,000 buses operate at 31% efficiency. These buses, which contribute to the city's traffic with empty seats, could also be the solution to its problem. Volt Lines is the only shuttle service provider that has both software as well as a transport service. This allows for optimal harmonization of the software and operation resulting in the creation of great experiences and services. Notable examples of these innovative services include DRT and Marketplace. DRT (short for Demand-Responsive Transport) was covered by a Harvard Business School case study that is being taught at the MBA school. Marketplace on the other hand allows companies to sell empty seats on their buses to neighboring passengers who are going in the same direction at the same time. Let's work together to shape the future of mobility. We're looking for Solution Center Associates (Part-time) to join us in shaping the future of urban transportation using the latest technologies. Responsibilities Customer Support Update passenger records on the platform based on customer requests (adding/removing passengers). Organize and record service time and route changes. Resolve day-to-day passenger and shuttle-related issues quickly and effectively. Record and categorize complaints using the internal ticketing system. Coordinate with relevant teams to develop and execute resolution plans. Follow up with customers to confirm satisfaction and closure. Analyze recurring complaints and recommend process improvements. Driver Support Conduct orientation training for newly onboarded drivers. Forward driver/vendor inquiries and complaints from the call center to relevant teams. Deliver refresher training for existing drivers when necessary. Follow up with the drivers on missing/expired documents. Sales Support Make outbound calls to potential clients to introduce Volt Lines' products and services. Analyze customer needs during conversations and refer qualified leads to the sales team. Requirements 3rd or 4th year undergraduate student in a relevant department Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment Benefits Innovative Environment : Volt Lines operates in a fast-growing, innovative industry, offering opportunities to work on impactful projects with cutting-edge technology. Career Growth : Volt Lines is committed to employee development, offering opportunities for career progression, training, and skill enhancement. Work-Life Balance : Volt Lines offers flexible working hours as well as a flexible paid time-off policy that promotes a healthy work-life balance. Startup Culture : Volt Lines embodies a startup environment that promotes creativity, autonomy, and innovation, providing employees with the freedom to take initiative and make decisions. Inclusive Work Culture: Volt Lines values different perspectives, background
