listings.title: Long Term Intern (Learning and Development)
listings.description: Founded in 1987, Hayat operates in the fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector, serving millions of consumers globally with 16 powerful brands. Hayatâ€™s portfolio includes Molfix and Bebem Natural in baby care; Molped in feminine care; Joly , Evony , and Goodcare in personal healthcare; Bingo , Gleen , Test , and Has in home care; Papia , Familia , Teno , and Focus in tissue paper; and Sholk in pet care. As the 4th largest branded diaper manufacturer in the world and the leading tissue producer in the Middle East, Eastern Europe, and Africa, Hayat employs over 10.000 people across its operations. Hayat operates 21 production facilities in 8 countries , including Turkiye, Egypt, Iran, Algeria, Russia, Nigeria, Pakistan, and Vietnam, and manages sales and distribution companies in Morocco, Bulgaria, Kenya, Thailand, and Malaysia. With its global operations structured under three main regions Eurasia & Americas, Middle East & Africa, and Asia Pacific Hayat reaches over 100 countries across 5 continents, bringing its brands into millions of homes worldwide. We are currently seeking a talented â€œLong Term Intern (Learning and Development)â€ to join our dynamic â€œHRâ€ team. If you want to be part of a diverse and vibrant community where your ideas can shape the future of the FMCG industry and your contributions can create a lasting global impact, then Hayat is waiting for you! QUALIFICATIONS Students at their 3rd year or 4th year of undergraduate program Excellent command of both written and spoken English Willing to learn and grow Able to support the HR Team team 3 days a week until High level of motivation to work in one of the leading companies in FMCG industry At Hayat, we conduct fair and inclusive hiring processes. We do not tolerate prejudice or discrimination based on language, religion, ethnicity, race, age or gender. Our recruitment process is solely based on the alignment with the job description, measurable and objective assessments, and competency-based interviews. listings.title: International Talent Programme (ITP)
listings.description:
ING International Talent Programme is a two year journey designed for bold and curious graduates who want to grow with purpose, explore their strengths, and build a meaningful international career. It offers the chance to work in a diverse, global environment while gaining the skills and confidence to shape your future.

Throughout the programme, youâ€™ll take part in impactful projects, experience agile ways of working, and develop yourself with the support of dedicated mentors and inspiring colleagues. Weâ€™ll guide you â€” but youâ€™ll define your direction. Thatâ€™s what â€œdo your thingâ€ truly means at ING.

If youâ€™re a Bachelorâ€™s or Masterâ€™s graduate with big ambitions, global mindset and a desire to grow into a future leader, this programme could be the perfect next step.

Interested?

What do we offer:

At ING we want your work to fulfill you in every way, and that is why we take care of even the smallest detail.

Here at ING , you are first yourself, then an INGâ€™er, therefore;

-
Learning and Growth Opportunities:
Access to global/local academies, top university programs, and various training sessions to boost your career
-
Flexible Work Environment:
14 days of welcome leave, hybrid working with flexible days, and remote work every 3rd week
-
Dynamic and Diverse Culture : A dynamic, innovative environment with opportunities to work on local and global projects, offering international experience
-
Social and Fun Work Environment:
Participate in social clubs and company events while enjoying a fun and supportive workplace
-
Comprehensive Benefits:
Life and health insurance, banking benefits, and a private pension plan to secure your future

To discover more on â€œDo Your Thingâ€ please visit;

If you think you have the following qualifications;

- Banking industry enthusiast with a strong academic record (Bachelorâ€™s/Masterâ€™s) and up to 2 years of experience,
- Fluent in English and Turkish, with strong analytical, strategic thinking, and problem-solving skills,
- Customer-focused, results-driven, and digitally savvy, with excellent communication and organizational abilities,
- Proactive, innovative, and a great team player who thrives in diverse environments,
- Experience in internships, volunteering, leadership, or a financial postgraduate degree is a plus.

If you think you are fit for a company which;

- Promotes freedom and fun at work with,
- Empowers customers and employees to stay a step ahead in life and in business, with digital innovative solutions,
-
Provides you a development path among four different tracks: Wholesale Banking, Finance, Technology, and Risk,

For more information about four different departments please visit our website:

https://careers.ing.com/en/expertise-teams

We are looking forward to meeting you!

listings.title: Global Build-On Internship Program
listings.description:
Are you ready to gain real business experience with a global summer internship at Ã‡imsa?

Founded in 1972 as a subsidiary of SabancÄ± Holding, Ã‡imsa operates with four integrated plants in Mersin, EskiÅŸehir, and Afyonkarahisar. In addition to grey cement, Ã‡imsa leads the sector with its production of white cement and CAC. Since the establishment of Cimsa Building Solutions BV in November 2020, the company has expanded internationally with facilities in Spain, the USA, and across Europe. Following the acquisition of Ireland-based Mannok Holdings in October 2024, Ã‡imsa has also started producing cement, cement-based products, insulation materials, and recycled plastic packaging.

The Global Build-On Internship Program offers university students the opportunity to gain hands-on experience during the summer period, understand business operations, and take their first professional steps within an international organization.

Why Global Build-On?

This program is designed to help you:

- Experience real business life during the summer period,
- Observe and contribute to daily operations,
- Understand how different functions collaborate,
- Strengthen your communication and professional skills,
- Gain exposure to an international corporate culture.

At Ã‡imsa, we offer you meaningful experience, structured guidance, and a strong start to your career journey.

Who Can Apply?

- 2nd, 3rd or 4th year undergraduate students and Masterâ€™s students,
- Students from Engineering, Business Administration, Economics and related departments,
- Candidates with a good command of English,
- Strong communicators and team-oriented individuals,
- Curious, responsible, and eager to learn.

Internship Period:

June â€“ July 2026

July â€“ August 2026

Application Deadline:

10 April 2026

listings.title: Cheers to Careers â€“ Short-Term Summer Internship Program 2026
listings.description:
Pernod Ricard is Conviviality

At Pernod Ricard, we are CrÃ©ateurs de ConvivialitÃ©.

Conviviality is the spirit that brings people together and shapes the way we work, collaborate and grow. It is at the heart of our culture and the experience people feel when they become part of Pernod Ricard.

With our employer value proposition â€œCraft Your Pathâ€ , we empower young talents to explore their potential, learn from industry experts and gain real business exposure.

About the Program

Pernod Ricard TÃ¼rkiye is excited to launch the Cheers to Careers Short-Term Summer Internship Program 2026.

This program offers university students a 2-month immersive internship experience where interns will work closely with experienced professionals, contribute to real business projects and gain exposure to the dynamic world of the FMCG industry.

Throughout the program, interns will be supported by an internship coach, collaborate with cross-functional teams and experience Pernod Ricardâ€™s unique culture of conviviality.

Program Timeline

Application Opening:
16 March 2026

Application Deadline:
17 April 2026

Interview Process:
27 April â€“ 8 May 2026

Internship Start:
1 June 2026

Internship End:
31 July 2026

Target Intern Profile

We are looking for curious and motivated university students who are eager to gain hands-on experience in a global FMCG environment.

Candidates are expected to:

- Be
2nd or 3rd year undergraduate students

- Be available for a
mandatory internship required by their university

- Have
university-provided internship insurance (mandatory)

- Demonstrate strong communication skills and curiosity to learn

- Be motivated to explore different business functions and collaborate with diverse teams

As Pernod Ricard TÃ¼rkiye operates within management functions , internships will be project-based and office-focused.

Departments Offering Internships

Internship opportunities will be available across the following departments:

- Marketing

- Sales

- Business Development & Trade Marketing

- Supply Chain

- Finance

- Human Resources

- Corporate Communications

- Global Tech (IT)

Orientation & Internship Experience

The program will begin with a Welcome & Orientation Session , where interns will be introduced to Pernod Ricard TÃ¼rkiye, our brands, culture and ways of working.

Throughout the internship, participants will:

- Work on
real business projects within their departments

- Collaborate with different teams and stakeholders

- Receive guidance from their
internship coach

- Gain insight into the
FMCG and premium spirits industry

Final Presentations

At the end of the program, interns will present their projects in a company-wide final presentation session , sharing their insights and learning journey with leaders and teams across Pernod Ricard TÃ¼rkiye.

Inclusive Culture

At Pernod Ricard, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation.

Our recruitment process is based on Pernod Ricard Leadership Attributes and technical capabilities, ensuring a fair and inclusive evaluation for all candidates.

Join us for a summer of learning, collaboration and conviviality.

Start crafting your path with Pernod Ricard TÃ¼rkiye.

listings.title: Graphic Design Intern
listings.description:
About the job

Company Description

At Otsimo, we believe every child deserves the chance to learn and grow to their fullest potential. We work for and believe in affordable and accessible education for special children. We are determined to improve our range and quality of practices until special education is available and accessible to each and every child with special needs such as autism, down syndrome, or other learning disorders. Our team is data and impact-driven has touched the lives of more than 3.000.000 children from all around the world. (https://otsimo.com/en/about-us/)

We create fun, accessible educational games and tools for children with special needs like autism, down syndrome, and other learning disorders. Our work has already made a difference in the lives of over a million children worldwide!
Join us and use your artistic talent to shape a more inclusive future in education.

Role Description

This is an open call for 2026 Summer.

This internship takes ~12 weeks (at least 3mos program).

We're looking for a passionate graphic design intern to join our team for the internship! This 12-week program will give you hands-on experience bringing a real Otsimo project to life. You'll:

- Create visually engaging designs: Produce graphics for product features, marketing materials, and digital platforms that make learning visually engaging and accessible.
- Collaborate to bring ideas to life: Work alongside designers, illustrators, and developers to transform concepts into effective visual solutions.
- Support brand consistency: Help maintain and develop Otsimoâ€™s visual identity across different touchpoints.
- Make a real impact: See your designs contribute to products that help children learn and thrive.

Required Skills

-
Currently enrolled in a full-time degree program

- Strong illustration portfolio: Show us your unique artistic style and ability to tell stories through images.

Preferred Qualifications

- Familiarity with design software such as Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, and/or Figma.
- Ability to communicate ideas effectively and work collaboratively within a team
- Passion for visual storytelling and a keen eye for detail

What We Offer

- Free healthy snacks and drinks, plus lunch card.
- Remote-first office policy
- Monthly happy hours and company events.
- Pet-friendly office.
- A challenging and fulfilling opportunity to join one of the most experienced teams in EdTech and help create a completely new kind of special school.

listings.title: Unity Developer Intern
listings.description:
Company Description

We work for and believe in affordable and accessible education for special children. We are determined to improve our range and quality of practices until special education is available and accessible to each and every child with special needs such as autism, down syndrome, or other learning disorders.
Our team is data and impact-driven has touched the lives of more than 1.000.000 children from all around the world.

Otsimo is actively building a team of people from many cultures and communities. Bringing together talented people with different perspectives and lived experiences makes our team stronger, so we can humbly build things that include, empower and inspire kids around the world.

Role Description

This is an open call for 2026 Summer.

This internship takes ~12 weeks (at least 3mos program).

During the internship, your responsibility will be to come up with a solution for our real project study that will be assigned to you. What we expect you to do in this project is to apply modern software engineering methods, leverage frameworks, and libraries, and collaborate with team members on projects and ideas.

Required Skills

Musts:

- Currently enrolled in a full-time degree program,
- Experience in Unity development,
- Programming skills in C#,
- Familiarity with game development principles.

Preferred qualifications:

- Solid foundation in computer science, with expertise in data structures, and algorithms,
- Strong object-oriented programming skills,
- Knowledge of Unix/Linux and experience with APIs,
- Experience with visual design concepts.

What We Offer

- Free healthy snacks and drinks, plus lunch card.
- Remote-first office policy
- Monthly happy hours and company events.
- Pet-friendly office.
- A challenging and fulfilling opportunity to join one of the most experienced teams in EdTech and help create a completely new kind of special school.

listings.title: Regional Supplier Development Manager
listings.description: Looking for a challenging new role and career development as a; SUPPLIER DEVELOPMENT MANAGER If the answer is yes, then Future Electronics wants to hear from you! We are currently looking to employ a Supplier Development Manager to join our EMEA team in Istanbul. We know our employees are what make us great, that's why we constantly develop and motivate our talent with world class organizational development to learn and achieve our goals together Successful applicants will gain a highly competitive salary, full training and a range of benefits. Job Outline: The prime responsibility of the EMEA Supplier Development Manager (SDM) is to ensure the successful execution of the business plan for their assigned focus suppliers. The SDM acts as the bridge to drive successful engagement between marketing, sales, engineering and the supplier. This will require driving a demand creation strategy with NPI and marcom activities on the key technologies of their suppliers. The SDM is the primary interface and facilitator to relationships between all key personnel at the suppliers and Future Electronics. The SDM will be expected to create the business plan, ensuring alignment with sales, marketing, engineering, the supplier and drive the execution with all associated parties. The primary metrics for success will be achieving the sales, demand creation and network share metrics set within the business plan. What the role involves: Hold principal accountability for success with assigned focus suppliers. Act as the primary lead within Future for the focus suppliers and ensure proactive alignment of distribution and market segment strategy. Develop a business plan with associated programmes, gain mindshare and acceptance from the organisation and drive success across the EMEA organisation. Control and communicate NPI to all relevant parties, including Sales, Marketing, Engineering and management. Communicate supplier strengths, product offerings, roadmaps and strategies effectively across the EMEA organisation in order to target specific / strategic customers where requested. Work with the technical and Marcom teams to develop and execute on demand creation programmes, including driving the launch and co-ordination of new product offerings. Provide enablers (demos & Marcom material) for product push programmes. Develop business relationships to a level that will support Future gaining the â€˜unfairâ€™ advantage through supplier relationship and matrix development. Develop tools to track and monitor performance, measure and communicate successes and drive improvements / product push programmes where needed. Do you have : Ideally educated to degree level with comprehensive knowledge of electronics and / or electronic distribution across a region or countries. Fully au fait with the sales and marketing working practises of Future and EMEA / international distributor. Commercially astute with strong credibility, able to win the â€œcustomerâ€ and develop long term customer relationship management. Able to think strategically and implement tactical plans as required. Strong service ethic able to work on own or as core support to others. Proactive, adaptable, organised and multi-tasking achiever. Excellent verbal and written communicator and presenter. About Us : Founded in 1968, Future Electronics is a worldwide leader in electronic components distribution and is recognized as one of the most respected and innovative companies in the industry today. Headquartered in Montreal and operating in over 160 locations in 40 plus countries around the world, Future Electronics has earned an impressive reputation for providing outstanding service and developing efficient, comprehensive global supply chain solutions. On April 2nd, 2024, Future Electronics joined WT Microelectronics. This complementary partnership is expected to generate strong synergies, enabling the combined company to deliver long-term, sustainable value to all stakeholders, including customers, suppliers, employees, and shareholders. Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion We are committed to Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion. We recruit the â€˜best person for the jobâ€™ regardless of age, race, colour, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, or disability creating an inclusive working environment and culture for all our employees. Show more Show less

listings.title: Global Category Supply Planning Executive
listings.description: Founded in 1987, Hayat operates in the fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector, serving millions of consumers globally with 16 powerful brands. Hayatâ€™s portfolio includes Molfix and Bebem Natural in baby care; Molped in feminine care; Joly , Evony , and Goodcare in personal healthcare; Bingo , Gleen , Test , and Has in home care; Papia , Familia , Teno , and Focus in tissue paper; and Sholk in pet care. As the 4th largest branded diaper manufacturer in the world and the leading tissue producer in the Middle East, Eastern Europe, and Africa, Hayat employs over 10.000 people across its operations. Hayat operates 21 production facilities in 8 countries , including Turkey, Egypt, Iran, Algeria, Russia, Nigeria, Pakistan, and Vietnam, and manages sales and distribution companies in Morocco, Bulgaria, Kenya, Thailand, and Malaysia. With its global operations structured under three main regions Eurasia & Americas, Middle East & Africa, and Asia Pacific Hayat reaches over 100 countries across 5 continents, bringing its brands into millions of homes worldwide. This role is pivotal in managing Hayat's global supply chain planning. The ideal candidate will possess a blend of analytical and communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with various functions at all levels. Success in this role will depend on strategic thinking, attention to detail, and proactive management of supply chain dynamics. This role will be part of the global supply chain organization and report to the global category supply planning group manager. Responsibilities Coordinate Planning and Analysis : Oversee planning efforts and analyze existing materials, finished goods and new product launches across our global manufacturing network. Collaboration and Capacity Enhancement : Establish and nurture strong relationships with global procurement, manufacturing, other global functions and local supply chain organizations. Supply Chain Strategy Development : Analyze supply chain constraints and assist in formulating strategies to improve operational capacity. Product Launch Coordination : Coordinate the timing of product launches and inventory ramp-up plans with launch planning teams. Customer Service Excellence : Maintain high service levels for customers, ensuring minimal to no stockouts. Inventory optimization: Optimize inventory levels for the assigned category and ensure inventory numbers meet the target inventory levels. Minimize slow moving inventory and obsolete stocks. Collaboration with local planners : Work closely with local production planners to ensure a consistent supply of finished goods and raw materials. Qualifications Minimum 7 years of â€œsupply chain planningâ€ experience Experience in FMCG or a similar industry, experience in hygiene industry is preferred. University degree in engineering Understanding of sales and operations planning process and experience in managing supply reviews as part of the S&OP cycle. Exceptional interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills, with the ability to engage with all organizational levels. Focus on the quality of work and continuous process improvement. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel and PowerPoint. Prior experience with SAP MM , MRP , PP/DS and SAP IBP (preferred). Fluency in English is required. What We Offer You Private Health Insurance Employee Shuttle Service Meal Voucher / Canteen Monthly Employee Support Package (Assortment of Hayat Products or Food Package) Monthly Internet Allowence Baby Diaper Support (For Children Up to 4 Years Old) and Diaper Support (for 1st Degree Relatives of Employees) 3 Days of Administrative Paid Leave Per Year Heating and Eid Allowance Once a Year Award & Recognition Programs FLEX: Flexible Benefits Program Wezone: Rewards and Recognition Program Seniority Award Support Heltia: Employee Assistance Program At Hayat, we conduct fair and inclusive hiring processes. We do not tolerate prejudice or discrimination based on language, religion, ethnicity, race, age or gender. Our recruitment process is solely based on the alignment with the job description, measurable and objective assessments, and competency-based interviews Get to know us Please visit us at the following addresses for any information you need: https://www.hayat.com/en-tr/ https://www.instagram.com/hayatkimya/ Clarification Text for Employee Candidate : https://www.hayat.com/aydinlatma-metni/aydinlatma-metni.htm Show more Show less

listings.title: Alumil NextGen Internship Program: Launch Your Career with Alumil
listings.description: Alumil NextGen is an internship program offering university students who are goal-oriented, eager to learn, and development-focused the opportunity to make a strong start to their careers. Through this program, participants gain real-world professional experience in a dynamic manufacturing and corporate environment across various departments, including Engineering, Architecture, Marketing, Human Resources, Logistics, Finance, and Sales . Throughout the program, students will become part of a team that develops innovative solutions for the construction industry, discover themselves with mentorship support , and expand their professional network . As a NextGen Intern, you will... Make an Impact You will contribute to Alumil Ege's business objectives by taking part in real business projects, and directly assume a role in tasks such as analysis, design, and process support. Gain Valuable Skills You will enhance both your technical and soft skills through hands-on learning and training sessions, strengthening areas like problem-solving, communication, teamwork, and project ownership . Build Your Professional Network You will connect with professionals from different departments and participate in team events, workshops, and company organizations. Learn from Your Mentors You will be paired with an experienced mentor who will guide you, support your development, and help you shape your career goals. Experience Different Fields You will gain insight into the various functions of our organization, discovering how interdepartmental collaboration works and how shared success is achieved. Innovate and Grow We encourage you to think creatively, share your ideas, and develop new approaches. Innovation starts with curiosity, and innovation drives progressâ€”your perspective is important to us. Job Description: Project Participation: Actively participate in real-world business projects and assignments, including tasks such as analysis, design, process improvement, and operational support Business Enhancement: Contribute to the development of innovative solutions for the construction industry with creative ideas and approaches Process Support: Fulfill supporting duties that help the assigned department's daily workflow and aid in achieving business goals Learning and Development: Attend technical and soft-skill training sessions, workshops, and internal company organizations offered throughout the program Communication and Collaboration: Communicate effectively with professionals from various departments and participate in cross-functional collaboration projects Mentorship: Seek guidance from the assigned mentor, establish and follow personal development goals based on regular feedback Requirements Must be a final-year student in an undergraduate or graduate program, OR a recent graduate who has graduated no more than 2 years ago . Must be studying/have graduated from one of the following or related disciplines: Engineering (Mechanical, Civil, Industrial, etc.) Architecture Business Administration / Economics Marketing / Communications Logistics / Supply Chain Human Resources Benefits Financial Terms: The NextGen Internship Program offers a paid internship opportunity . Interns will be subject to the company's established compensation policies for the duration of their tenure. Social Advantages: Our interns are entitled to full access to all social rights and benefits offered to Alumil employees throughout the program (e.g., meal services, transportation etc.). At ALUMIL we are building excellence every day. Alumil has more than 30 years of experience in the production of architectural aluminium systems and 3.000 employees. We are one of the most advanced companies globally owning state-of-the art production lines in our 12 factories in Europe. Alumil, is a family-owned business that also embraces all of its employees like family. Our culture's primary focus is on integrity, team spirit, initiative, loyalty and "meraki" (spirited work). ALUMIL opposes any discrimination, unequal treatment and violation of human rights and ensures the provision of equal opportunities at work. When hiring and evaluating employees, it does not discriminate based on race, gender, religion, age, disability, sexual orientation, nationality, political beliefs or social status. Show more Show less

listings.title: Human Resources Intern
listings.description: Are you eager to build a career in Human Resources and ready to apply your theoretical knowledge in a real business environment? Join our HR team! The CMA CGM Group , a global leader in shipping and logistics, serves more than 420 ports around the world on five continents. Present in 160 countries through its network of more than 400 offices and 750 warehouses, the Group employs more than 155,000 people worldwide, including 4,000 in Marseilles where its head office is located. With its subsidiary CEVA Logistics, a world leader in logistics, and its air freight division CMA CGM AIR CARGO, the CMA CGM Group is continually innovating to offer its customers a complete and increasingly efficient range of new shipping, land, air, and logistics solutions. As a result of expanding our organization, we are looking for a Human Resources Intern to join our fast-growing and challenging organization. Please note: This internship is only open to students with a mandatory internship requirement approved by their university. Key Responsibilities: Support recruitment and onboarding processes, Assist with CV screening and interview scheduling, Take part in ongoing HR projects, Support internal communication and documentation tasks within the department, Assist with departmental purchases and invoice processing. What Weâ€™re Looking For: 3rd or 4th-year university students from relevant departments (Psychology, Business Administration, HR Management, etc.), University approval for a mandatory internship is required, Good command of English, both verbal and written, Available to work full-time in the summer period at the office in Istanbul, Strong communication skills, Problem-solving and adaptability in a dynamic work environment, Eager to learn. What We Offer: Real hands-on experience in a corporate environment A dynamic and supportive team Breakfast: every Monday morning Transportation (Shuttle) Meal allowance Happy hours Valuable learning and networking opportunities for your future career Come along on CMA CGM's adventure! Now it's your chance to get to know us from within a workplace full of warmth, friendship and support. listings.title: Manufacturing Excellence Intern
listings.description: Looking for Your Dream Job? Join The Magnum Ice Cream Company! Manufacturing Excellence Intern Location: Corlu Scope: Local The Magnum Ice Cream Company (formerly a division of Unilever) is now a standalone, independent, EUR 7.9 billion publicly listed company. Weâ€™re on a mission to create the ultimate snacking company. A place where growth, innovation, founder and ownership mentality, and performance are cultivated and rewarded. And where we craft extraordinary ice cream experiences - transforming ordinary moments into lasting memories. Because we know, life tastes better with ice cream. ABOUT ICE CREAM: Life Tastes Better With Ice Cream The Magnum Ice Cream Company is all about growth. Growing our business. Growing our customersâ€™ businesses. Growing our peopleâ€™s careers. Growth begins with empowerment. So we free our people to be innovative, responsible entrepreneurs, driven and equipped to give our consumers more amazing products and unforgettable moments â€“ and having fun doing it. With 19.000 expert ice cream colleagues and iconic brands like Wallâ€™s, Cornetto and Ben & Jerryâ€™s, loved in 76 countries, we are the worldâ€™s largest Ice Cream company leading the industry. We have been taking pleasure seriously for more than 100 years, serving happiness with every lick or scoop of ice cream for generations. We are investing to unlock the full growth potential of The Magnum Ice Cream Company as a standalone entity, once we separate from Unilever, which is planned to happen before the end of 2025. listings.description: Looking for Your Dream Job? Join The Magnum Ice Cream Company! Konya IC Factory Process Engineer Function : Supply Chain Reports to: Production Manager | Supply Chain IC SU Konya IC Scope: Local Location: Konya IC Terms & Conditions: Full Time The Ice Cream division at Unilever is preparing to stand on its own, aiming to become an independent, EUR 8.3 billion publicly listed company by the end of 2025. Weâ€™re on a mission to create the ultimate snacking company. A place where growth, innovation, founder and ownership mentality, and performance are cultivated and rewarded. And where we craft extraordinary ice cream experiences - transforming ordinary moments into lasting memories. Because we know, life tastes better with ice cream. ABOUT THE MAGNUM ICE CREAM COMPANY: The Magnum Ice Cream Company is all about growth. Growing our business. Growing our customersâ€™ businesses. Growing our peopleâ€™s careers. Growth begins with empowerment. So we free our people to be innovative, responsible entrepreneurs, driven and equipped to give our consumers more amazing products and unforgettable moments â€“ and having fun doing it. We are investing to unlock the full growth potential of The Magnum Ice Cream Company as a standalone entity, once we separate from Unilever, which is planned to happen before the end of 2025. We dream big but keep things simple to act fast. If you want to grow with us, make an impact, and shape the future of Ice Cream , this is the place for you! JOB PURPOSE Here is the place where you can bring your purpose to life with the work that you do â€“ creating a better business and a better world. A Great Supply Chain is the key winning formula for every day's business world and supply chain is the spine of success in every company. If you are looking for a job where you will find a delightful combination of strategy, creativity and execution that changes the score of the game by creating value. Join us to add your mark to the list of â€œMade by Youâ€. We are looking for a Process Engineer to work in our Factory located in Konya. Developing and define the working parameters for the processes. Focusing on development for error proof and full proof methodologies to eliminate the need for alignment and adjustment. Developing and supporting production team to create and update WCM activity boards. Advising and supporting in cooperation with WCM coordinator, production staff on the application of WCM techniques. Participating in conducting internal meetings aimed at developing measures to prevent and eliminate non-conformities and reduce losses. Responsible for the validation of all process improvements and the establishment of revised basic conditions on the line. A core deliverable is to eliminate losses and wastes and increase the stability of the line to support the transition of traditional line teams to semi-autonomous teams. Experiences & Qualifications WHAT YOU WILL NEED TO SUCCEED University degree preferably in Mechanical Engineering Engineering with a strong understanding of manufacturing processes, safety, productivity and quality assurance. Ability of handling several projects simultaneously and meeting tight deadlines. Working effectively with different departments. Proficiency in MS Office applications Preferably no military obligation for male candidates. Skills Fluent in English & Turkish Good communication skills. Analytical thinking skills. Problem solving skills. Leadership You are energized by delivering fantastic results. You are an example to others â€“ both your results and your resilience. You are constantly on the lookout for better ways to do things, engaging and collaborating with others along the way. As an individual you are the one responsible for your own wellbeing and delivering high standards of work. You must also focus on the Consumer and what they need. You are humble and have your head up, looking around to interpret evidence and data smartly, spot issues and opportunities to make things better. WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT: A unique mix of global scale & start-up spiritâ€”â‚¬8.3bn powerhouse with an agile, entrepreneurial mindset. Weâ€™re building a new high growth Ice Cream company from the ground up. Career without limitsâ€”42 markets, international opportunities, and fast tracked growth. A performance-driven cultureâ€”Freedom to act, disrupt and grow â€“ your success is measured by impact. A company that celebrates joy, innovation, and purposeâ€”We create extraordinary careers, just like our ice cream creates extraordinary moments. listings.title: Manufacturing Engineer - Painting & Automation
listings.description: Unlock Your Potential at Eaton: Where Diversity Drives Innovation. No matter where you come from, your aspirations matter to us. We believe in the power of different perspectives, experiences, and backgrounds to ignite innovation and drive success. We're in the business of empowering people, and that includes you. Your journey here is not just about a job; it's about a shared vision for a brighter, more sustainable future. Our site in Ankara offers a fantastic learning environment and a vibrant array of career opportunities spanning medium-voltage product design, manufacturing, and sales. Combining agile R&D with internationally recognized test facilities, our team has the resources, speed, and flexibility to adapt to any customer demand. We support utility providers and renewable energy projects, as well as commercial and residential buildings, with innovative and cost-effective solutions. We aspire to be an all-encompassing medium-voltage center of excellence. Our team is currently growing fast as we ramp up our ability to support crucial trends like Industry 4.0, electrification, and the energy transition. Our work is enabling more agile and energy-efficient manufacturing, as well as driving forward electrification and the transition to smarter power grids dominated by renewables â€” helping to create a more sustainable future. Our employees are passionate about continuously improving and reaching their full potential, as well as enabling our facility to do the same. Weâ€™re always seeking dedicated and adaptable people with skills spanning engineering, sales, marketing, R&D, and manufacturing. If youâ€™re curious and love innovating to solve new challenges, join us! We are looking for a Manufacturing Engineer - Painting & Automation to be joining our team in the Transformer Business Unit. This role is based on site in Temelli. The Manufacturing Engineer plays a critical role in driving operational excellence across Eatonâ€™s Ankara facility. This position supports the development and optimization of manufacturing processes, contributes to new product introductions (NPI), and leads continuous improvement initiatives. Key Responsibilities Process Optimization & Continuous Improvement Analyze and improve manufacturing processes to reduce waste, cycle time, and setup duration Apply Lean, Six Sigma, and TPM methodologies to enhance productivity and standardization Lead root cause analysis and implement mistake-proofing solutions using tools like 8D, A3, DOE, and 5-Why Support for New Product Introduction (NPI) & Strategic Projects Participate in the industrialization of new products using Eatonâ€™s NPI framework Contribute to Strategic Transfer Projects (STP) and Value Analysis/Value Engineering (VAVE) initiatives Ensure manufacturability through Design for Manufacturing (DFM) principles and accurate cost modeling Manufacturing Technology & Digital Transformation Support implementation of CAD/CAM systems, NC programming, and inspection tools Assist in layout optimization using simulation tools and contribute to Industry 4.0 initiatives Evaluate and recommend new technologies aligned with capital investment strategies Safety, Quality & Compliance Promote and adhere to safety standards including lockout/tagout and ergonomic practices Participate in PFMEA reviews and implement robust error-proofing methods Ensure compliance with Eatonâ€™s quality policies and environmental regulations Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with operations, supply chain, and quality teams to resolve issues and improve performance Provide technical support during customer visits and internal audits Contribute to capacity planning and layout improvements to support plant growth Deploy Eaton Business System and Eaton Continuous Improvement System tools to drive performance improvement and knowledge development. Qualifications Bachelorâ€™s Degree in Electrical, Mechanical, Materials, or Industrial Engineering (or equivalent). 5 years of professional applied experience in Transformers / Switchgear Manufacturing / Engineering Services. A minimum of 3â€“5 years of experience in manufacturing engineering or operations with exposure to painting technology and automation. Experience in transformer or switchgear manufacturing preferred. Familiarity with DFMEA, PFMEA, DFM, PPAP, and controlled processes. Exposure to ISO 9001, ISO 14001, AS9100, or QS 9000 standards is a plus. Proven experience with Lean and Six Sigma tools. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Experience with CAD/CAM systems and manufacturing simulation tools. Knowledge of Industry 4.0 technologies and digital workflows. Results-driven with a continuous improvement mindset. Strong communicator with the ability to present technical ideas clearly. Collaborative team player who thrives in cross-functional environments. Adaptable, optimistic, and proactive in facing challenges. Fluent level of English language is required, due to the international exposure. What We Offer Competitive compensation and benefits package Challenging projects in dynamic collaborative team We make your aspirations matter â€“ Eaton encourages internal promotion, whenever possible We make your growth matter - We invest in our employees for the long term â€“ not just with salary and benefits, but with ongoing learning and development opportunities made available through Eaton University We make your contributions matter - reliability, safety, efficiency, and sustainability are at the core of our dedication to improving peopleâ€™s lives and the environment through power management technologies. We make your wellbeing matter â€“ We put your health and safety first. Wellness at Eaton is more than a program, itâ€™s about changing the environment by offering the right tools to help empower employees to make that happen. We are committed to ensuring equal employment opportunities for job applicants and employees. Our recruitment processes use balanced selection criteria and avoid unlawful discrimination against applicants on the basis of their age, colour, disability, marital status, national origin, gender, gender identity, genetic information, race or racial origin, religion, sexual orientation or any other status protected or required by law. listings.title: Project Engineer
listings.description: Job Responsibilities 1. Conduct site surveys, construction drawing reviews, and material verification/management for overseas projects, and establish standardized technical documentation in compliance with project requirements. 2. Responsible for monitoring and guiding third-party installation teams in the mechanical installation of equipment, completing the installation, parameter optimization, and joint debugging of electrical systems to ensure compliance with delivery standards. Also supports project management work to guarantee the project is accepted on time and meets quality requirements. 3. Serve as the primary technical liaison between clients, partners, and internal teams. Conduct regular communications on project progress and service updates to ensure information transparency. 4. Deliver product operation and maintenance training to clients. Provide on-site production support as needed, and perform after-sales service work such as equipment operation and maintenance, troubleshooting, hotline response and technical guidance in accordance with contract requirements. 5. Strengthen on-site client relationships to continuously improve customer satisfaction. 6. Timely handle and accurately execute other work tasks assigned by departments and superiors. Qualifications 1. Academic background in Mechatronics or Electrical Engineering. 2. Valid electrician license (construction/maintenance type is preferred) with more than 2 years of experience in mechatronic equipment troubleshooting and repair. 3. Proficiency in interpreting electrical schematics, skilled in operating test instruments, and familiar with PLC programming (e.g., Siemens S7-1200/1500). 4. Excellent problem-solving skills, customer requirement - oriented mindset, ability to optimize workflows proactively. 5. Flexible and efficient teamwork ability, strong time management skills, able to accept urgent tasks and strictly abide by safety regulations. 6. English proficiency as a working language, readiness for international business travel, and familiar with cross-cultural communication skills. Show more Show less

listings.title: Overseas Technical Procurement Engineer

TÃ¼rkiyeâ€™s gateway to the world , the airport continues its operations as a significant global hub , connecting the Asian, African, and European continents. Built on an area of 76.5 million square meters, the airport has a terminal serving 90 million passengers, with a planned total capacity of 200 million. Ä°GA Istanbul Airport, honored by Skytrax as the â€œBest Airport of 2023,â€ â€œWorldâ€™s Best Airport Shopping Experience,â€ and â€œMost Family-Friendly Airport,â€ and by ACI as Europeâ€™s â€œBest Airport Over 40 Million Passengers,â€ â€œMost Enjoyable,â€ and â€œCleanest,â€ continues to set global standards. At Ä°GA, we believe every individual is unique, yet united by a shared vision and passion. Guided by our strategic goals and strong work ethic, we strive to move forward every day.If you aspire to be part of this proud and unique journey, we are looking for a talented â€˜â€™Overseas Technical Procurement Engineerâ€™â€™ to join us at the â€œmeeting pointâ€ of the world. Your Journey Begins With a Unique Role âœˆï¸ Conduct market research for machinery, equipment and spare parts procurement in domestic and international markets and develop the most suitable solutions based on organizational needs, Establish and develop relationships with domestic and international suppliers, Collect quotations, conduct technical and commercial evaluations and analyses, Lead supplier negotiations and finale contract processes, Monitor customs and foreign trade processes, manage import and export operations in compliance with legal regulations, Manage purchase and procurement processes via SAP, Prepare cost analyses and reports to provide data-driven insights. Proficient in research and procurement processes in international markets, Knowledgeable about foreign trade regulations and customs processes, Experience in quotation analysis, supplier negotiation and contract management Excellent command of MS Office and SAP programs, Advanced level of competency in English, Willing to engage in communication and sharing among colleagues, Always setting the bar higher for oneself and the team with a continuous improvement mindset, What Awaits You in Ä°GA ğŸ’ You can benefit from Private Health Insurance, You can enjoy the privileges of your foreign language skills with Foreign Language Allowance! listings.title: Process Engineer
listings.description: About Us PHINIA: Advancing sustainability today, powering a cleaner tomorrow. PHINIA is an independent, market-leading, premium solutions and components provider with over 100 years of manufacturing expertise and industry relationships, with a strong brand portfolio that includes DELPHIÂ®, DELCO REMYÂ® and HARTRIDGEâ„¢. With over 12,500 employees across 43 locations in 20 countries, PHINIA is headquartered in Auburn Hills, Michigan, USA. At PHINIA, we Provide fuel systems, electrical systems, and aftermarket products and solutions of the highest quality â€” developed and manufactured responsibly â€” that are designed to enhance efficiency and reduce the environmental impact of vehicles, industrial machinery, and other applications. In doing so, we contribute to a cleaner tomorrow, treat our people and surrounding communities with respect, and hold ourselves accountable to robust ethical standards. Our Culture PHINIA promotes and cultivates an inclusive culture and diverse perspectives, strives to maintain its reputation for excellence, thrives on the power of collaboration, and fosters the development of our talented employees. We believe in making a positive impact through our business and actions, and we take our collective responsibility seriously. Career Opportunities We believe in building a brighter tomorrow for our employees as well as our customers and encourage you to learn about our long history, strong culture, new technologies, and future vision. We offer a strong local presence and interesting global opportunities. Join us on this shared journey toward a brighter tomorrow. Job Purpose KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Implement personal development and training plans for the technician team under their responsibility. Research and evaluate new technologies with potential for implementation. Achieve ME (Manufacturing Engineering) performance metrics. Prepare technical specifications for machines and equipment in line with company standards and required capacity and cycle time targets. Prepare process documentation (PFD, PFMEA, Control Plans, and Manufacturing Drawings) on time and at the required quality level. Use company project management tools in manufacturing engineering activities. Support quality, volume, cost, and continuous improvement projects by applying 6 Sigma, Shainin, and other problem-solving methodologies. Provide support for internal audits, customer visits, and certification audits. Manage prototype manufacturing of the product under their responsibility, and prepare lessons learned and gate charts. Manage process changes smoothly in compliance with company procedures. What We Offer We provide compensation and benefits programs intended to attract, motivate, reward, and retain an incredibly talented, globally diverse workforce at all levels within our organization. Our compensation programs are informed by market data and business needs, and we are committed to providing equitable and competitive compensation. We are committed to providing our team with quality and competitive benefit programs, including health and well-being resources, family-centric policies, and an agile workplace program, where not precluded by collective bargaining agreements or national statutory plans. Plans are benchmarked for competitiveness and value. We provide formal development opportunities at all levels and stages of employee careers. These opportunities are delivered in a variety of formats to make our portfolio of solutions agile, sustainable, and scalable to support our employees in developing the skills needed to succeed. What We Believe Product Leadership - Innovation that brings value to our customers Humility - Seeking out diverse perspectives and working collaboratively Inclusivity - Recognizing our differences makes us stronger; we are bold and intentional Net-Zero - Committed to energy efficiency, waste reduction and beneficial reuse Integrity - Taking responsibility for our decisions and doing what is right Accountability - Taking ownership of our actions and driving results SAFETY You will consistently hear us say Safety First! We are committed to continually improving our strong safety performance supporting the health and wellness of our employees! We also believe employee health and safety is everyoneâ€™s responsibility. We encourage safety learning and collaboration to help employees understand and follow applicable safety policies, standards, and procedures and identify opportunities to minimize or eliminate risk. Work is expected to be conducted in a manner that stresses the importance of preventing incidents and illnesses, including attending all required safety meetings and trainings. It is expected that all incidents, near misses, and unsafe conditions are immediately reported to the direct manager, Human Resources, or Safety Representative. EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY PHINIA is an equal employment opportunity employer such that all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. VISA SPONSORSHIP PHINIA does provide sponsorship for employment visa status based on business need. However, for this role, applicants must be currently authorized to work on a full-time basis, in the country where the position is currently based. NO UNAUTHORIZED REFERRALS FROM RECRUITERS & VENDORS Please note that PHINIA does not seek or accept unsolicited resumes or offers from third party recruiters or staffing agencies associated with any published or unpublished employment opportunities. Any unsolicited information sent to PHINIA will be considered as unencumbered and free from any fee or charge whatsoever. Only members of our Human Resources Team have the authority to engage or authorize recruiting services, which must be agreed upon before the unsolicited resume or offer is received. GLOBAL TERMS OF USE AND PRIVACY STATEMENT Carefully read the PHINIA Privacy Policy before using this website. Your ability to access and use this website and apply for a job at PHINIA are conditioned on your acceptance and compliance with these terms. Please access the linked document by clicking here, select the geographical area where you are applying for employment, and review. Before submitting your application you will be asked to confirm your agreement with the terms. Career Scam Disclaimer: PHINIA makes no representations or guarantees regarding employment opportunities listed on any third-party website. To protect against career scams, job applicants should take the necessary precautions when interviewing for and accepting employment positions allegedly offered by PHINIA. Applicants should never provide their national ID numbers, birth dates, credit card numbers, bank account information or other private information when communicating with prospective employers or responding to employment opportunities online. Job applicants are invited to contact PHINIA through PHINIAâ€™s website to verify the authenticity of any employment opportunities. Advancing sustainability today, powering a cleaner tomorrow. Join us on this shared journey to a brighter tomorrow. For more information about PHINIA, please visit www.phinia.com. Show more Show less

listings.title: Process Engineer - Utilities & Tank Farm (GME) - Relocate to Saudi Arabia Permanent Expat Family Relocation Package
listings.description: Please note that this role is based in Saudi Arabia on a permanent, residential basis. Job Req ID: 17355 Description Aramco energizes the world economy. Aramco occupies a special position in the global energy industry. We are one of the worldâ€™s largest producers of hydrocarbon energy and chemicals, with among the lowest Upstream carbon intensities of any major producer. With our significant investment in technology and infrastructure, we strive to maximize the value of the energy we produce for the world along with a commitment to enhance Aramcoâ€™s value to society. Headquartered in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and with offices around the world, we combine market discipline with a generationsâ€™ spanning view of the future, born of our nine decades experience as responsible stewards of the Kingdomâ€™s vast hydrocarbon resources. This responsibility has driven us to deliver significant societal and economic benefits to not just the Kingdom, but also to a vast number of communities, economies, and countries that rely on the vital and reliable energy that we supply. We are one of the most profitable companies in the world, as well as amongst the top five global companies by market capitalization. Overview We are seeking a Process Engineer with extensive hands-on expertise in Utilities processes to join our Operation Engineering Solutions Department (OESD) under Global Manufacturing (GM) Excellence Organization. OESD's technical team is responsible for providing high-quality, multi-disciplinary support to GM operating facilities (Refineries, NGLF, and petrochemical plants) within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and globally. Your primary is to provide in-depth process engineering and operations expertise to monitor, optimize, and drive performance improvement programs for Utilities, including both remote and on-site technical advisory support and benchmarking of utilities. Key Responsibilities As successful candidate you will be required to perform the following: Conduct regular performance reviews of existing Utilities through key performance indicators (KPIs) representing optimum operability, profitability, reliability, and process safety. Develop and present quarterly benchmarking reports with actionable improvement recommendations. Provide hands-on support in implementing performance improvement initiatives, demonstrating measurable results in closing identified gaps from quarterly benchmarking reports. Lead benchmarking and best practices exchange workshops among Global Manufacturing facilities (wholly-owned and affiliates) and facilitate knowledge sharing and continuous improvement. Deliver on-demand remote and field technical advisory support, including process design, troubleshooting, start-up/shutdown activities, and capacity test runs. Provide detailed service reports for each support activity, highlighting facility needs, actions taken, results, and lessons learned. Collaborate with Global Manufacturing facilities engineers to identify and implement cost optimization or process improvement initiatives, focusing on "low-hanging fruits" that require minimal capital investment, through operational adjustment or design modification. Conduct internal assessments on Utilities using Saudi Aramco engineering standard references and highlight any deviations or better standards applied by affiliates for endorsement to facility management or Saudi Aramco Central Engineering. Act as the overall coordinator for cascading and driving Global Manufacturing and Corporate programs for Utilities in operating facilities, enabling their execution and effective monitoring. Participate in Business Plan development, coordinate with stakeholders for capital investments on assigned Utilities and support techno-economic evaluations. Evaluate and recommend new Utilities technologies, coordinate with Central Engineering to facilitate field deployment. Minimum Requirements You will hold a Bachelor's degree in Chemical Engineering or related specialization. An advanced degree is preferred. You will have a minimum of 15 years of total Utilities unit experience (process engineering and operations) with at least 10 years of hands-on experience in handling refineryâ€™s utilities. You will have experience in water desalination and boilers for steam generation You will be able to optimize various utility systems, with specific examples of performance improvements achieved. You will have in-depth knowledge of major equipment suppliers and package units for Utilities. You will have a proven track record of conducting technical troubleshooting, start-up, shutdown, and capacity test runs for utility systems. You will have strong proficiency in data analysis and visualization tools specific to Utilities performance metrics. You will also have strong management and coordination skills, with experience in stakeholder management. Working environment Our high-performing employees are drawn by the challenging and rewarding professional, technical and industrial opportunities we offer, and are remunerated accordingly. At Aramco, our people work on truly world-scale projects, supported by investment in capital and technology that is second to none. And because, as a global energy company, we are faced with addressing some of the worldâ€™s biggest technical, logistical and environmental challenges, we invest heavily in talent development. We have a proud history of educating and training our workforce over many decades. Employees at all levels are encouraged to improve their sector-specific knowledge and competencies through our workforce development programs â€“ one of the largest in the world. Show more Show less

listings.title: Project Engineer
listings.description: Job Description Are You Ready to Make It Happen at MondelÄ“z International? Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It With Pride. You assist in planning, developing and execution of capital projects, technical development, feasibility perspectives of engineering-related activities in the supply chain and capital-expense project execution to support growth, world-class manufacturing and productivity with the highest level of quality, safety and environmental requirements. You help ensure sure that MondelÄ“z business and innovation processes in project management and MondelÄ“z standards in execution are followed. How You Will Contribute The role supports capital project execution, and you will be accountable for the quality of the results and execution of capital projects using MondelÄ“z standards, and business and innovation processes in project management. You will help define and deliver the capital and technical agenda during the development phase of capital investments with stakeholders so that accurate budgets, plans and fit-for-purpose technical solutions are provided and develop the capital budget according to the contract and forecast cash flow. You will also help ensure that applicable engineering developments and standards are properly implemented, and support the development and implementation of state-of-the-art processes and equipment strategies to optimize resources, harmonize assets and rollout best practices. What You Will Bring A desire to drive your future and accelerate your career. You will bring experience and knowledge in: Engineering or operations Working effectively and under pressure to meet deadlines Analysis Project management Proposing and providing the best technical solutions for all processes undertaken. Performing pre-engineering and technical project management processes with cost calculations, expenditure planning, and CPS-time plans. Preparing capital allocation requests according to relevant guidelines and regulations and managing the approval processes (AR Request and Approval Processes). Ensuring investment projects are executed within the determined financial budget, in line with defined clear objectives and company procedures, and coordinating relevant project teams. Collaborating with the purchasing department to obtain and evaluate bids and present the most suitable technical solution proposal, ensuring cost-effective project solutions. Collaborating with the Purchasing and Legal departments for contract preparation, providing technical requirements and all relevant details. Recommending selected suppliers to the Purchasing Department to obtain the best technology. After AR approval, coordinating the design, procurement, installation, and commissioning processes of new equipment, ensuring the timely and vertically launched (VSU) commissioning of new technical equipment and building projects. To ensure project execution within the agreed timeline and budget, and to prepare all necessary reports within the area of responsibility and monitor the project implementation process. Coordinates the procurement, installation, and commissioning of new projects and equipment in collaboration with manufacturing and R&D. Ensures that project costs are monitored and reported in accordance with company guidelines and requirements. As the project leader, ensures that all meetings and necessary communications with the project team are conducted effectively and closely monitors the equipment and material procurement, testing, and shipment processes throughout all phases of the project. Takes proactive measures against potential delays and risks. Proposes and implements ideas to reduce inventory and costs, and to improve production volume and export potential. Together with the Business Development Manager, transforms business needs into engineering projects aimed at improving and increasing the efficiency of production processes in the facility/factory. Relocation Support Available? No Relocation support available Business Unit Summary We value our talented employees, and whenever possible strive to help one of our associates grow professionally before recruiting new talent to our open positions. If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply! Our people make all the difference in our succes MondelÄ“z International is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation or preference, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Excited to grow your career? We value our talented employees, and whenever possible strive to help one of our associates grow professionally before recruiting new talent to our open positions. If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply! IF YOU REQUIRE SUPPORT TO COMPLETE YOUR APPLICATION OR DURING THE INTERVIEW PROCESS, PLEASE CONTACT THE RECRUITER Job Type Regular Project Engineering Science & Engineering Show more Show less

listings.title: GET Rotational Engineer
listings.description: We supply the sustainable and innovative paints and coatings that our customers, communities â€“ and the environment â€“ are increasingly relying on. Our world class portfolio of brands â€“ including Dulux, International, Sikkens and Interpon â€“ is trusted by customers around the globe. Weâ€™re active in more than 150 countries and have set our sights on becoming the global industry leader. Itâ€™s what youâ€™d expect from a pioneering paints company thatâ€™s committed to science-based targets and is taking genuine action to address globally relevant challenges and protect future generations. For more information please visit www.akzonobel.com. Â© 2023 Akzo Nobel N.V. All rights reserved. Job Purpose The Graduate Engineering Trainee (GET) development program opens the way to an international career in the Integrated Supply Chain area and prepares you to play key roles at AkzoNobel in managerial or expertsâ€™ positions. The GET program was created for engineers who are looking for opportunities to gain knowledge and experience in an international environment as to engineering career and after ready to take up professional challenges abroad. After completion of the first stage of the program in Turkey, you will be deployed to another factory within AkzoNobel in EMEA region. At the end of the graduate program, based on readiness and performance, you will be assigned a final role in discussion with the regional ISC organization and your coaches from your host sites. Qualifications We are looking for a Graduate Engineering Trainee in AkzoNobel-Marshall located in DilovasÄ±. University degree in Mechatronic, Electrical & Electronics, Industrial and Chemical Engineering preferable universities. Eager to have a career in manufacturing and supply chain organization. Excellent communication & interpersonal skills Flexible and able to adapt to learn AkzoNobel methods and philosophy Excellent command of English Good knowledge of MS Office tools No restriction for travelling in and out of Country Willing to relocate to other countries on completion of the 24 months GET program. Willing to live in Kocaeli or Ä°stanbul Asia Job Description Actively participates in process optimization and investment projects. Identifies improvement opportunities and designs solutions. Tracks and controls project budgets and timelines to ensure project completion within the approved limits. Monitors projects compliance to applicable codes, practices, QA/QC policies, performance standards and specifications. Cooperates and communicates daily with project members and stakeholders to interpret their needs and requirements and provides assistance and technical support. Uses of statistical analysis and appropriate business excellence project management processes to identify areas of significant improvement and develops plans to realize the benefits. Investigates root cause of process failures, develops solutions for non-conforming products and implements improvement plans. Uses data analysis and problem-solving methodologies to maximize plant and equipment effectiveness to achieve optimum life cycle cost of production equipment. We offer: 24-month development program (12 months in our factory in Turkey and the next 12 months of development in other AkzoNobel's factory in Europe). Opportunities to start career in multinational company and in different functional areas, such as Production, Engineering, Quality, Planning. Permanent job in one of the AkzoNobel's factories in EMEA, after successfully completing the GET program. Possibility of gaining knowledge through the practice.
